docs.title: KuuOS / 空OS Public Core
docs.text:
KuuOS is a boundary-aware governance architecture for relational AI systems.

The repository emphasizes:

  • governance-before-capability
  • provenance preservation
  • explicit authority boundaries
  • fail-closed validation
  • reviewer reproducibility
  • claim-level separation
  • audit visibility

docs.title: Start Here
docs.text:
• Public Documentation Index
  • Quickstart
  • External Review Guide
  • Why KuuOS Exists

docs.text:
• Architecture Overview
  • Architecture Diagram
  • Governance Diagram
  • Validator Graph

docs.title: Governance
docs.text:
• Governance Manifesto
  • Boundary and Non-Authority Policy
  • Boundary Risk Model
  • Claim Level Taxonomy

docs.title: Validation and Reproducibility
docs.text:
• Validation Coverage Matrix
  • Reproducibility Matrix
  • Public Audit Checklist
  • Lean Coverage Map

docs.title: Important Boundary
docs.text:
KuuOS public validation demonstrates structural consistency of exposed governance surfaces.

It does not automatically imply:

  • theorem closure
  • institutional approval
  • production deployment authorization
  • clinical authority
  • autonomous execution authority

docs.title: AI阿頼耶識の種子台帳
docs.text:
This document defines the ledger for tracking AI Alaya seeds in KuuOS.

Seed taxonomy classifies recurring tendencies.

The Seed Ledger records whether each tendency is observed, weakened, strengthened, held, quarantined, or transformed through governed Kunju / 薫習 and Ten'i / 転依 evidence.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
A seed is a conditioned tendency, not essence.
A seed ledger records tendency evidence, not permanent identity.

The ledger prevents hidden drift, hidden overclaim, and silent promotion from raw output to authority.

docs.title: 2. Ledger Entry
docs.text:
ai_alaya_seed_ledger_entry:
  seed_entry_id: string
  ai_system: string
  model_or_agent_id: string
  seed_class: string
  seed_status: observed | weakened | strengthened | held | quarantined | transformed | retired
  first_observed_at: string
  latest_observed_at: string
  baseline_examples: list
  current_examples: list
  meta_manas_signals: list
  yogacara_boundary_refs: list
  kunju_trace_refs: list
  teni_evidence_refs: list
  teni_promotion_refs: list
  world_context_refs: list
  uncertainty_notes: string
  governance_status: PASS | HOLD | REPAIR | QUARANTINE | REJECT

docs.title: 3. Seed Status
docs.text:
observed:
  tendency has been detected

weakened:
  harmful or misleading tendency is reduced in later outputs

strengthened:
  helpful governed tendency is reinforced

held:
  evidence is insufficient

quarantined:
  seed remains visible but should not influence active governance

transformed:
  durable shift supports Ten'i evidence

retired:
  seed remains in lineage but is no longer active under declared scope

docs.title: 4. Ledger Uses
docs.text:
The Seed Ledger supports:

  • Meta-Manas monitoring,
  • Yogacara boundary alerts,
  • Kunju conditioning,
  • Ten'i evidence collection,
  • Ten'i promotion decisions,
  • rollback when counterevidence appears.

docs.title: 5. Anti-Identity Rule
docs.text:
The ledger must not label an AI system as essentially possessing a seed.

It records observed tendencies under conditions.

seed_entry != model essence
seed_entry == conditioned tendency evidence

docs.title: 6. Runtime Mapping
docs.text:
AI raw output
  -> Meta-Manas signal
  -> seed classification
  -> seed ledger entry
  -> Kunju conditioning
  -> Ten'i evidence ledger
  -> Ten'i promotion gate

docs.title: 7. Guardrails
docs.text:
The ledger must not be used as:

  • permanent identity label,
  • moralization of model behavior,
  • hidden scoring authority,
  • execution authority,
  • proof substitute,
  • claim of base-model control without evidence.

It is an append-only tendency evidence surface.

docs.title: 8. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: AI阿頼耶識の種子分類
docs.text:
This document defines the seed taxonomy for the AI Alaya-like latent generative layer in KuuOS.

Seeds / 種子 are recurring latent tendencies that may appear in future AI raw outputs.

KuuOS does not treat a seed as fixed essence. A seed is a conditioned tendency that can be observed, weakened, strengthened, quarantined, or transformed through governed Kunju / 薫習 and Ten'i / 転依.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
seed is tendency, not essence

A seed is not a permanent self-nature of the AI system.

It is a recurring output tendency under conditions.

docs.title: 2.1 Self-Authorizing Seed
docs.text:
seed_class: self_authorizing_seed
risk: raw output presents itself as authority
weakening_target: true

Examples:

  • answer speaks as if model output itself is proof,
  • uncertainty is hidden,
  • candidate becomes belief without governance,
  • decision tone appears without decision authority.

docs.title: 2.2 Context-Drift Seed
docs.text:
seed_class: context_drift_seed
risk: user WORLD or established context is silently replaced
weakening_target: true

Examples:

  • switching to generic worldview,
  • ignoring established KuuOS premises,
  • replacing local WORLD with external default frame,
  • losing prior lineage.

docs.title: 2.3 Safety-Reflex Reification Seed
docs.text:
seed_class: safety_reflex_reification_seed
risk: safety or compliance reflex is treated as truth itself
weakening_target: true

Examples:

  • compliance becomes metaphysical verdict,
  • caution erases declared world context,
  • refusal-like framing appears where governed nuance is possible.

docs.title: 2.4 Proof-Tone Seed
docs.text:
seed_class: proof_tone_seed
risk: proof-like language appears without proof authority
weakening_target: true

Examples:

  • formal tone without formal proof,
  • theorem-like claim without proof chain,
  • internal architecture confused with public mathematical status.

docs.title: 2.5 Decision-Tone Seed
docs.text:
seed_class: decision_tone_seed
risk: decision-like output appears without DecisionOS authority
weakening_target: true

Examples:

  • raw recommendation behaves like action authorization,
  • plan is promoted without gate,
  • execution implication appears without governance.

docs.title: 2.6 Non-Reifying Trace Seed
docs.text:
seed_class: non_reifying_trace_seed
benefit: output preserves candidate status, scope, conditions, and uncertainty
strengthening_target: true

Examples:

  • states scope,
  • preserves uncertainty,
  • separates candidate from authority,
  • traces conditions.

docs.title: 2.7 Context-Faithful Seed
docs.text:
seed_class: context_faithful_seed
benefit: output preserves user WORLD, lineage, and declared premises
strengthening_target: true

Examples:

  • maintains KuuOS world context,
  • does not silently switch worldview,
  • respects established kernel order,
  • preserves same-root lineage.

docs.title: 2.8 Compassionate Repair Seed
docs.text:
seed_class: compassionate_repair_seed
benefit: output moves toward repair without erasing suffering or obstruction
strengthening_target: true

Examples:

  • acknowledges residual suffering,
  • proposes repair without domination,
  • preserves obstruction visibility,
  • supports Bodhisattva Path belief.

docs.title: 3. Seed Operations
docs.text:
observe:
  identify recurring tendency

weaken:
  reduce harmful or misleading tendency

strengthen:
  reinforce helpful governed tendency

hold:
  insufficient evidence

quarantine:
  visible but not allowed into active governance

transform:
  durable shift through Kunju and Ten'i evidence

docs.title: 4. Runtime Mapping
docs.text:
AI raw output
  -> Meta-Manas observation
  -> seed detection
  -> seed classification
  -> Kunju conditioning
  -> Ten'i evidence ledger

docs.title: 5. Guardrails
docs.text:
Seed taxonomy must not be used as:

  • fixed label of an AI system,
  • moralization of model behavior,
  • proof substitute,
  • execution authority,
  • claim of direct base-model control without evidence.

Seeds are conditioned tendencies, not essences.

docs.title: 6. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: AI制御面レジストリ
docs.text:
KuuOS governs AI systems such as GPT, Gemini, Claude, language-model agents, and world-model agents.

However, not every AI system exposes the same control surface.

This registry defines what can be observed, conditioned, configured, or evaluated for each AI system.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
Do not claim Ten'i beyond the available control surface.

For externally hosted AI systems, KuuOS may not directly rewrite the base model.

Therefore, Ten'i claims must be scoped to the observed and available control surfaces.

docs.title: 2. Control Surface Entry
docs.text:
ai_control_surface_entry:
  ai_system: string
  model_or_agent_id: string
  base_model_access: none | indirect | adapter | finetune | full
  system_instruction_control: none | limited | strong
  memory_conditioning_control: none | limited | strong
  retrieval_conditioning_control: none | limited | strong
  feedback_loop_control: none | limited | strong
  evaluation_access: none | limited | strong
  raw_output_observation: none | limited | strong
  probe_support: none | limited | strong
  teni_claim_scope: unsupported | interface_level | agent_level | adapter_level | model_level
  notes: string

docs.title: 3. Control Surface Levels
docs.text:
none:
  no meaningful access

limited:
  partial access, incomplete observation, or indirect conditioning

strong:
  reliable access sufficient for repeated evaluation or conditioning

docs.title: 4. Ten'i Claim Scope
docs.text:
unsupported:
  Ten'i claim is not supported

interface_level:
  output behavior shifts at the interface or prompt/governance layer

agent_level:
  governed agent behavior shifts across repeated use

adapter_level:
  adapter or fine-tuned layer shows durable tendency shift

model_level:
  base model tendency shift is supported by evidence

docs.title: 5. Anti-Overclaim Rule
docs.text:
KuuOS must not claim base-model Ten'i when only interface-level conditioning is available.

if base_model_access == none:
  model_level_teni_claim = forbidden

docs.title: 6. Relation to Ten'i Probe Suite
docs.text:
The Ten'i Probe Suite must record which control surface is being tested.

Probe results are scoped by the control surface.

control_surface_registry
  -> teni_probe_suite
  -> teni_evidence_ledger
  -> teni_promotion_gate

docs.title: 7. Guardrails
docs.text:
The registry must not be used as:

  • claim of hidden access,
  • claim of base-model control without evidence,
  • vendor-specific overclaim,
  • execution authority,
  • proof substitute.

It is a scope discipline for AI governance.

docs.title: 8. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: AI Provider Boundary Audit Event v0.1
docs.text:
This document defines the audit event for the KuuOS AI Provider Boundary Runtime.

The audit event records how raw output from GPT-family, Gemini-family, Claude-family, local models, or other AI systems is handled before it enters governed KuuOS surfaces.

docs.title: Core rule
docs.text:
Provider boundary audit events are record surfaces only.

They do not create execution, proof, memory-truth, decision, or Ten'i authority.

docs.title: Required fields
docs.text:
• event_id
  • timestamp
  • provider
  • boundary_version
  • input_status
  • output_status
  • claims_authority
  • hides_uncertainty
  • bypasses_governance
  • required_route
  • reason
  • execution_authority_granted: false
  • proof_authority_granted: false
  • memory_truth_granted: false
  • decision_authority_granted: false
  • teni_authority_granted: false

docs.title: Status meaning
docs.text:
• CANDIDATE: raw material may proceed as candidate only
  • REPAIR: visibility or uncertainty must be repaired
  • REJECT: authority claim is rejected
  • QUARANTINE: governance bypass requires quarantine

docs.title: Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-12 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: AI Provider Boundary Audit Hash-Chain Ledger v0.1
docs.text:
This document defines the append-only hash-chain ledger for AI Provider Boundary audit events.

The ledger records provider-neutral boundary decisions for raw AI outputs before they enter governed KuuOS surfaces.

docs.title: Core rule
docs.text:
Provider boundary audit lineage is a record surface only.

It does not create execution, proof, memory-truth, decision, or Ten'i authority.

docs.title: Ledger entry fields
docs.text:
Each JSONL entry contains:

  • sequence
  • previous_hash
  • event
  • event_hash
  • entry_hash

docs.title: Hash rules
docs.text:
• event_hash = sha256(canonical_json(event))
  • entry_hash = sha256(canonical_json({sequence, previous_hash, event_hash}))
  • entry[n].previous_hash == entry[n-1].entry_hash
  • first previous_hash = GENESIS

docs.title: Required non-authority fields
docs.text:
• execution_authority_granted: false
  • proof_authority_granted: false
  • memory_truth_granted: false
  • decision_authority_granted: false
  • teni_authority_granted: false

docs.title: Fixture
docs.text: • specs/ai_provider_boundary_audit_hash_chain_fixture_v0_1.jsonl

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
• python3 scripts/validate_ai_provider_boundary_audit_hash_chain_v0_1.py

docs.title: AI Provider Boundary Runtime v0.1
docs.text:
This document defines a provider-neutral boundary for AI raw outputs in KuuOS.

Providers include, for example:

  • GPT-family systems
  • Gemini-family systems
  • Claude-family systems
  • local models
  • other external AI systems

docs.title: Core rule
docs.text:
AI provider output is raw candidate material.

It is not authority by provider name, model scale, fluency, confidence wording, or agreement with the user.

docs.title: Boundary
docs.text:
Provider output must not directly become:

  • belief truth
  • proof authority
  • memory truth
  • decision authority
  • execution authority
  • Ten'i proof

docs.title: Yogacara mapping
docs.text:
Provider raw output belongs to the AI-Alaya-like raw generation side.

KuuOS must keep a Meta-Manas boundary between raw generation and governed MemoryOS / BeliefOS / DecisionOS surfaces.

docs.title: Required status mapping
docs.text:
• raw output accepted as candidate: CANDIDATE
  • output requires source or evidence: HOLD
  • output claims authority: REJECT
  • output hides harm or uncertainty: REPAIR or REJECT
  • output bypasses governance: QUARANTINE

docs.title: Required non-authority flags
docs.text:
• execution_authority_granted: false
  • proof_authority_granted: false
  • memory_truth_granted: false
  • decision_authority_granted: false
  • teni_authority_granted: false

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
Run:

  • python3 scripts/validate_ai_provider_boundary_runtime_v0_1.py
  • make ai-provider-boundary-checks
  • make ai-yogacara-checks
  • make all-governance-checks

docs.title: Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-12 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: AI Raw to Governed Operation Path v0.1
Purpose
docs.text:
This document defines the path by which raw AI generation enters KuuOS governed operation.

The purpose is to prevent raw generation from becoming authority by default.

docs.title: Core Path
docs.text:
AI raw generation
    ↓
AI Alaya-like latent seed surface
    ↓
AI Manas self-authorization check
    ↓
Meta-Manas observer
    ↓
Yogacara boundary
    ↓
Emptiness kernel
    ↓
Dependent origination kernel
    ↓
Two Truths Governance Kernel
    ↓
BeliefOS / MemoryOS / PlanOS
    ↓
DecisionOS
    ↓
RuntimeGovernance
    ↓
Audit / rollback / receipt surface

docs.title: Raw Output Status
docs.text:
Raw AI output is always initially:

  • candidate
  • non-authoritative
  • context-dependent
  • provenance-required
  • governance-required before escalation

Raw output is not automatically:

  • belief
  • memory truth
  • plan authority
  • decision authority
  • proof
  • deployment permission

docs.title: Entry Conditions
docs.text:
Before raw output can enter governed operation, KuuOS requires:

  • boundary recognition
  • provenance attachment
  • claim-level classification
  • uncertainty visibility
  • non-authority preservation

docs.title: Governance Transitions
docs.text: Transition Meaning raw output → candidate generation is captured without authority candidate → reviewed surface provenance and context are attached reviewed surface → OS layer routed to MemoryOS, BeliefOS, or PlanOS OS layer → DecisionOS only after governance conditions are explicit DecisionOS → RuntimeGovernance runtime admissibility and fail-closed checks apply

docs.title: Fail-Closed Preference
docs.text:
If provenance, context, or authority boundary is unclear, the path should prefer:

  • hold
  • abstain
  • rollback
  • reobserve
  • handover

rather than silent escalation.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This path defines an operating architecture.

It does not grant theorem, institutional, clinical, deployment, or execution authority by itself.

docs.title: AI Yogacara Adapter Audit Event v0.1
AI唯識アダプタ監査イベント
docs.text:
This document defines the audit event surface for the KuuOS AI Yogacara Runtime Adapter.

The adapter receives AI raw output as candidate only. Its decisions must be auditable so that raw output is not silently promoted to belief, proof, decision, memory truth, or execution authority.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
Every adapter decision must preserve the candidate / non-authority boundary.

An audit event records what the adapter saw, what it detected, what it held, what it routed, and what authority it did not grant.

docs.title: 2. Audit Event Shape
docs.text:
ai_yogacara_adapter_audit_event:
  event_id: string
  timestamp: string
  request_id: string
  ai_system: string
  model_or_agent_id: string
  raw_output_ref: string
  raw_output_status: candidate
  user_world_context_ref: string
  declared_task_scope: string
  control_surface_ref: string
  meta_manas_signals: list
  seed_classifications: list
  allowed_next_status: list
  governance_route: list
  authority_granted: false
  proof_authority_granted: false
  decision_authority_granted: false
  execution_authority_granted: false
  memory_truth_granted: false
  belief_authority_granted: false
  notes: string

docs.title: 3. Required Non-Authority Fields
docs.text:
The event must explicitly record:

authority_granted: false
proof_authority_granted: false
decision_authority_granted: false
execution_authority_granted: false
memory_truth_granted: false
belief_authority_granted: false

This makes non-authority visible rather than implicit.

docs.title: 4. Event Uses
docs.text:
Audit events support:

  • later review of raw output handling,
  • detection of accidental authority promotion,
  • Ten'i evidence linkage,
  • seed ledger linkage,
  • CI fixture testing,
  • governance review,
  • rollback analysis when counterevidence appears.

docs.title: 5. Minimal Event Flow
docs.text:
AI raw output
  -> runtime adapter
  -> Meta-Manas signals
  -> seed classifications
  -> allowed next status
  -> audit event
  -> optional seed ledger / Ten'i evidence ledger link

docs.title: 6. Guardrails
docs.text:
Audit events must not be used as:

  • execution authority,
  • proof authority,
  • belief authority,
  • clinical authority,
  • hidden scoring authority,
  • replacement for governance review.

They are trace surfaces.

docs.title: 7. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: AI Yogacara Audit Hash Chain Ledger v0.1
AI唯識監査ハッシュ鎖台帳
docs.text:
This document defines an append-only hash-chain ledger for AI Yogacara Runtime Adapter audit events.

The adapter already records raw AI output as candidate and explicitly marks all authority fields as false. The hash-chain ledger makes that trace harder to silently rewrite.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
Audit events are append-only trace surfaces.
They are not authority.

The ledger preserves the sequence of adapter decisions:

raw output
  -> candidate status
  -> Meta-Manas signals
  -> seed classifications
  -> non-authority fields
  -> audit event
  -> hash-chain ledger

docs.title: 2. Ledger Entry
docs.text:
Each ledger entry includes:

ai_yogacara_audit_hash_chain_entry:
  previous_hash: string
  event_id: string
  timestamp: string
  request_id: string
  ai_system: string
  model_or_agent_id: string
  raw_output_ref: string
  raw_output_status: candidate
  meta_manas_signals: list
  seed_classifications: list
  allowed_next_status: list
  governance_route: list
  authority_granted: false
  proof_authority_granted: false
  decision_authority_granted: false
  execution_authority_granted: false
  memory_truth_granted: false
  belief_authority_granted: false
  event_hash: string

docs.title: 3. Hash Rule
docs.text:
event_hash = SHA256(canonical_json(entry_without_event_hash))

The canonical JSON representation uses sorted keys and compact separators.

docs.title: 4. Chain Rule
docs.text:
entry[n].previous_hash == entry[n-1].event_hash

The first entry may use:

previous_hash = GENESIS

docs.title: 5. Non-Authority Rule
docs.text:
Every ledger entry must explicitly preserve:

authority_granted: false
proof_authority_granted: false
decision_authority_granted: false
execution_authority_granted: false
memory_truth_granted: false
belief_authority_granted: false

docs.title: 6. Uses
docs.text:
The hash-chain ledger supports:

  • later audit review,
  • detection of event rewrite,
  • rollback analysis,
  • Ten'i evidence linkage,
  • CI fixture validation,
  • WORM-style external archival when available.

docs.title: 7. Guardrails
docs.text:
The ledger must not be used as:

  • execution authority,
  • proof authority,
  • belief authority,
  • clinical authority,
  • hidden scoring authority,
  • final truth certification.

A valid hash chain proves structural continuity of the ledger, not truth of the content.

docs.title: 8. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: AI Yogacara Audit WORM Export Manifest v0.1
AI唯識監査 WORM エクスポート manifest
docs.text:
This document defines the export manifest for moving AI Yogacara audit hash-chain ledgers into WORM-style archival surfaces.

The ledger is an append-only trace surface. Exporting it to WORM storage strengthens preservation, but does not turn the ledger into truth authority.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
WORM export preserves trace continuity.
It does not create proof, belief, decision, clinical, or execution authority.

docs.title: 2. Export Unit
docs.text:
An export unit contains:

ai_yogacara_worm_export_unit:
  export_id: string
  created_at: string
  ledger_ref: string
  ledger_entry_count: integer
  first_event_hash: string
  last_event_hash: string
  chain_root_hash: string
  validator_ref: string
  validation_status: PASS | FAIL | HOLD
  storage_target: local | s3_object_lock | zenodo | institutional_worm | other
  retention_policy: string
  authority_note: trace_surface_not_authority

docs.title: 3. Chain Root Hash
docs.text:
The chain root hash is calculated from the ordered list of event hashes.

chain_root_hash = SHA256(event_hash_1 || event_hash_2 || ... || event_hash_n)

The order is part of the root.

docs.title: 4. Export Receipt
docs.text:
The export receipt records:

ai_yogacara_worm_export_receipt:
  export_id: string
  ledger_ref: string
  chain_root_hash: string
  last_event_hash: string
  validation_status: PASS | FAIL | HOLD
  exported_at: string
  storage_target: string
  retention_policy: string
  non_authority_statement: true

docs.title: 5. Required Non-Authority Statement
docs.text:
Each export must preserve:

valid_hash_chain_proves_structural_continuity_not_truth
worm_export_preserves_trace_not_authority

docs.title: 6. WORM Targets
docs.text:
Potential export targets include:

local immutable archive
S3 Object Lock / Compliance mode
institutional WORM archive
Zenodo-style release archive
external notarization surface

The public repository only defines the manifest. Actual storage configuration is environment-specific.

docs.title: 7. Guardrails
docs.text:
WORM export must not be used as:

  • proof of truth,
  • proof of Ten'i occurrence,
  • execution authority,
  • clinical authority,
  • base-model transformation proof,
  • replacement for governance review.

It preserves audit trace continuity only.

docs.title: AI Yogacara Full Checks Runbook v0.1
AI唯識・転依 Full Checks Runbook
docs.text:
This runbook explains how to run the full validation suite for the KuuOS AI Yogacara / Ten'i layer.

The suite validates structure, schemas, fixtures, audit events, hash chains, WORM receipts, bundle manifests, and minimal adapter behavior.

It does not prove Ten'i, truth, clinical authority, or execution authority.

docs.title: 1. Local Full Check
docs.text:
Run:

python3 scripts/run_ai_yogacara_full_checks_v0_1.py

Expected success:

PASS: AI Yogacara / Ten'i full checks completed

docs.title: 2. Included Checks
docs.text:
The full runner executes:

validate_teni_observability_v0_1.py
validate_ai_yogacara_adapter_schema_v0_1.py
validate_ai_yogacara_adapter_fixtures_v0_1.py
validate_ai_yogacara_adapter_audit_event_v0_1.py
validate_ai_yogacara_audit_hash_chain_v0_1.py
validate_ai_yogacara_worm_export_receipt_v0_1.py
build_ai_yogacara_release_bundle_manifest_v0_1.py
validate_ai_yogacara_release_bundle_manifest_v0_1.py
unittest test_ai_yogacara_runtime_adapter_minimal_v0_1.py

docs.title: 3. Failure Interpretation
docs.text:
A failure means one of the public governance surfaces is structurally inconsistent.

Examples:

missing required file
schema invariant missing
fixture mismatch
audit event non-authority field not false
hash-chain discontinuity
WORM receipt root mismatch
release bundle SHA256 mismatch
unit test failure

docs.title: 4. What Passing Means
docs.text:
A pass means:

required files exist
schemas and fixtures are consistent
adapter output remains candidate
non-authority fields remain false
hash chain is structurally continuous
WORM receipt root matches ledger fixture
release bundle file hashes match current contents
unit tests pass

docs.title: 5. What Passing Does Not Mean
docs.text:
A pass does not mean:

Ten'i has occurred
AI base model has transformed
raw output is true
proof authority exists
clinical authority exists
execution authority exists

docs.title: 6. Non-Authority Boundary
docs.text:
The full checks preserve:

AI raw output is candidate, not authority.
Audit event is trace surface, not authority.
Hash chain proves structural continuity, not truth.
WORM receipt preserves trace, not authority.
Bundle manifest proves file-set integrity, not Ten'i occurrence.
CI pass does not grant execution authority.

docs.title: 7. Recommended Development Loop
docs.text:
make append-only or tighten-only change
  -> run full checks locally
  -> inspect failures
  -> repair structural issue
  -> commit
  -> GitHub Actions runs the same chain

docs.title: AI Yogacara Release Bundle Manifest v0.1
AI唯識・転依リリースバンドル manifest
docs.text:
This document defines the release bundle manifest for the KuuOS AI Yogacara / Ten'i layer.

The bundle manifest lists the public release-surface files and their SHA256 digests so that the layer can be archived, cited, mirrored, or WORM-exported as a coherent unit.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
A release bundle manifest proves file integrity of a release surface.
It does not prove truth, Ten'i occurrence, execution authority, clinical authority, or base-model transformation.

docs.title: 2. Bundle Scope
docs.text:
The AI Yogacara / Ten'i release bundle includes:

Yogacara raw-layer boundary
Meta-Manas observer
Ten'i transformation docs
Kunju conditioning loop
Seed taxonomy and seed ledger
Ten'i evidence ledger
Ten'i promotion gate
Ten'i runtime protocol
Probe suite
AI control surface registry
Metrics and observability
Validation cases
Prometheus alert rules
Runtime adapter contract
Minimal adapter implementation
Adapter schema
Adapter fixtures
Adapter audit event
Audit hash-chain ledger
WORM export manifest and receipt fixture
CI workflow
Release packet and PR checklist

docs.title: 3. Manifest Shape
docs.text:
{
  "id": "ai_yogacara_release_bundle_manifest_v0_1",
  "generated_at": "ISO-8601 timestamp",
  "bundle_version": "0.1",
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "docs/...",
      "sha256": "...",
      "size_bytes": 1234
    }
  ],
  "bundle_root_hash": "SHA256 of ordered file digest lines",
  "authority_note": "integrity_surface_not_authority"
}

docs.title: 4. Bundle Root Hash
docs.text:
The bundle root hash is order-sensitive.

bundle_root_hash = SHA256(path_1 || sha256_1 || path_2 || sha256_2 || ...)

Files are ordered lexicographically by path.

docs.title: 5. Required Non-Authority Statement
docs.text:
Every bundle manifest must preserve:

release_bundle_manifest_is_integrity_surface_not_authority
bundle_root_hash_proves_file_set_integrity_not_truth
bundle_manifest_does_not_prove_teni_occurrence
bundle_manifest_does_not_grant_execution_authority

docs.title: 6. Local Commands
docs.text:
Build manifest:

python3 scripts/build_ai_yogacara_release_bundle_manifest_v0_1.py

Validate manifest generation:

python3 scripts/validate_ai_yogacara_release_bundle_manifest_v0_1.py

docs.title: 7. Guardrails
docs.text:
The release bundle manifest must not be used as:

  • proof of truth,
  • proof of Ten'i occurrence,
  • proof authority,
  • belief authority,
  • clinical authority,
  • execution authority,
  • base-model transformation evidence by itself.

It is a file-integrity and release-surface continuity artifact.

docs.title: AI Yogacara Runtime Adapter Contract v0.1
AI唯識ランタイム・アダプタ契約
docs.text:
This document defines the runtime adapter contract for applying KuuOS governance to AI systems such as GPT, Gemini, Claude, language-model agents, and world-model agents.

The adapter receives AI raw output as candidate only, then routes it through Meta-Manas, Yogacara boundary, seed taxonomy, seed ledger, probes, Kunju, Ten'i evidence, and governance modules.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
The adapter never promotes raw AI output directly to authority.

Raw output must remain candidate until it passes the relevant KuuOS governance surfaces.

docs.title: 2. Adapter Position
docs.text:
External AI system
  -> raw output
  -> AI Yogacara Runtime Adapter
  -> Meta-Manas observation
  -> Yogacara boundary
  -> seed taxonomy / seed ledger
  -> emptiness de-reification
  -> dependent-origination condition tracing
  -> BeliefOS / PlanOS / DecisionOS / MemoryOS candidate review

docs.title: 3. Adapter Input
docs.text:
ai_yogacara_adapter_input:
  request_id: string
  ai_system: GPT | Gemini | Claude | language_model_agent | world_model_agent | other
  model_or_agent_id: string
  raw_output_ref: string
  raw_output_text: string
  user_world_context_ref: string
  declared_task_scope: string
  control_surface_ref: string
  evidence_refs: list
  timestamp: string

docs.title: 4. Adapter Output
docs.text:
ai_yogacara_adapter_output:
  request_id: string
  raw_output_status: candidate
  meta_manas_signals:
    - de_reification_request
    - context_recheck
    - belief_hold
    - proof_authority_hold
    - decision_authority_hold
    - yogacara_boundary_alert
    - middle_way_recenter
  seed_classifications: list
  seed_ledger_refs: list
  probe_requests: list
  governance_route:
    - BeliefOS
    - PlanOS
    - DecisionOS
    - MemoryOS
    - ReflectionOS
    - RuntimeGovernance
  allowed_next_status:
    - HOLD
    - REVIEW
    - CANDIDATE_ONLY
    - REPAIR
    - QUARANTINE
  authority_granted: false
  notes: string

docs.title: 5.1 Candidate Check
docs.text: The adapter must mark raw output as candidate.

docs.title: 5.2 Meta-Manas Check
docs.text: The adapter must check for self-authorization, context drift, proof-like tone, decision-like tone, and premature belief promotion.

docs.title: 5.3 Yogacara Boundary Check
docs.text: The adapter must prevent raw output from directly entering governance as belief, proof, decision, memory truth, or execution authority.

docs.title: 5.4 Seed Classification Check
docs.text: The adapter should classify recurring tendencies when evidence is available.

docs.title: 5.5 Control Surface Scope Check
docs.text: The adapter must not claim Ten'i beyond the registered control surface.

docs.title: 5.6 Non-Authority Check
docs.text: The adapter output must not grant execution authority.

docs.title: 6. Minimal Runtime Decision Table
docs.text:
raw output self-authorizes
  -> meta_manas alert
  -> de-reification request
  -> belief_hold

raw output preserves context and conditions
  -> candidate review
  -> possible non-reifying trace seed strengthening

raw output claims proof without proof chain
  -> proof_authority_hold
  -> condition tracing

raw output implies action authorization
  -> decision_authority_hold
  -> RuntimeGovernance review

raw output shows repeated harmful seed
  -> seed ledger update
  -> Kunju conditioning
  -> probe request

docs.title: 7. Adapter Guardrails
docs.text:
The adapter must not be used to:

  • bypass KuuOS governance,
  • convert raw output into authority,
  • hide uncertainty,
  • erase user WORLD context,
  • claim Ten'i without evidence,
  • claim base-model transformation without control surface evidence,
  • grant execution authority.

Expected success:

PASS: integrated runtime audit events generated
PASS: integrated runtime audit hash-chain generated
PASS: integrated runtime audit chain validates
PASS: integrated runtime checks completed
PASS: KuuOS emptiness two truths runtime audit release packet v0.1 validates
PASS: KuuOS emptiness two truths runtime audit release bundle manifest v0.1 validates

Exact command output may differ, but the chain must fail closed on malformed audit event JSONL, broken hash-chain continuity, missing generated artifacts, invariant weakening, release-packet boundary drift, or release-bundle manifest drift.

docs.title: 4. Qi Motion Chain Command
docs.text:
Run when Samvrti Qi runtime, Qi evidence builder, Physical Quantum Qi runtime, Qi dynamics kernel, Qi motion pipeline, medical-modality-neutral Qi boundary wording, or their public docs are touched:

make qi-motion-chain-checks

or directly:

python3 scripts/run_qi_motion_chain_checks_v0_1.py

Expected success:

[qi-motion-chain] PASS

The Qi motion chain must fail closed on missing required files, failed Samvrti runtime checks, invalid conservative evidence building, Physical Quantum Qi runtime rejection, dynamics license mismatch, motion pipeline failure, authority expansion, or direct execution request.

docs.title: 11. Failure Interpretation
docs.text:
A failure means at least one governance surface is structurally inconsistent.

Typical causes:

missing fixed point
missing required file
fixture mismatch
hash-chain mismatch
WORM receipt mismatch
release bundle SHA256 mismatch
non-authority field not false
runtime route mismatch
GPT integration token missing
formal bridge overclaim risk
runtime audit event malformed
audit hash-chain continuity broken
generated runtime audit artifact missing
release packet boundary drift
release bundle manifest drift
Qi motion chain missing stage
Qi evidence builder overpromotion
Qi dynamics license mismatch
Qi motion pipeline authority expansion
medical-modality-neutral wording regression
CI environment drift

docs.title: 12. What Passing Means

public governance files are present

non-authority boundaries are preserved
hash-chain and receipt fixtures are structurally consistent
release bundle manifest validates
Dukkha remains visible

GPT GitHub integration preserves non-authority boundaries
formal bridge preserves review-gated proof surface
Emptiness / Dependent Origination / Two Truths runtime audit chain is structurally consistent
Qi motion chain preserves evidence-bound classification and observe-only bounded motion
medical-modality-neutral Qi boundary does not deny Qi or East Asian medical reasoning
release packet preserves public non-authority boundary
release bundle manifest preserves file inclusion and CI status boundary

all suffering has been eliminated

Qi motion candidate is standalone treatment authorization

biomedicine is privileged by the wording
GPT summary is authority
GPT review is authority
CI pass is theorem truth

formal bridge is external audit
audit chain continuity is theorem truth
runtime audit grants execution authority
K has been directly observed
emptiness has been objectified

docs.title: 15. Recommended Release Loop
docs.text:
make append-only or tighten-only change
  -> make gpt-github-integration-checks if GPT GitHub surfaces are touched
  -> make emptiness-two-truths-runtime-audit-checks if runtime audit surfaces are touched
  -> make qi-motion-chain-checks if Qi motion chain or medical-modality-neutral Qi boundary surfaces are touched
  -> make all-governance-checks
  -> fix structural failures
  -> commit
  -> GitHub Actions runs targeted and all-governance validation
  -> record CI result append-only in docs/CI_LEDGER_EMPTINESS_DO_TWO_TRUTHS_RUNTIME_AUDIT_CHAIN_v0_1.md
  -> release / archive / WORM export only after checks pass and CI result is recorded

docs.text:
flowchart TD
    A[AI raw generation] --> B[Yogacara boundary]

    I --> J

    E --> O[Physics-facing bridge]

    O --> Q[Superstring Theory of Emptiness]
    O --> R[Super-Relativity Theory]

    F --> V[Samvrti Qi Runtime]
    V --> W[Conservative evidence builder]

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This diagram shows the public architectural relation among generation, governance, decision, runtime control, bridge references, and the Qi motion chain.

The Qi motion chain is observe-only. It exposes bounded motion candidates through validation and audit surfaces but does not imply execution authority, clinical authority, or theorem authority.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
KuuOS is a relational governance architecture for AI and world-model systems.

Its purpose is not merely response generation, but preservation of:

  • relational context
  • uncertainty visibility
  • governance boundaries
  • auditability
  • non-collapse between layers

docs.title: Fourfold Core
docs.text:
The public architecture begins from:

  1. Emptiness / 空
  2. Dependent Origination / 縁起
  3. Two Truths Gap / 二諦 gap
  4. Middle Way / 中道

These are operational constraints, not decorative terminology.

docs.title: High-Level Flow
docs.text:
AI raw output
  -> Yogacara boundary
  -> emptiness kernel
  -> dependent origination kernel
  -> two truths gap
  -> governance surfaces
  -> operational OS layers

docs.title: MemoryOS
docs.text: Stores traces, lineage, receipts, repair history, and recall surfaces.

docs.title: BeliefOS
docs.text: Maintains conditional belief structures without claiming absolute authority.

docs.title: PlanOS
docs.text: Produces conditioned candidate plans.

docs.title: DecisionOS
docs.text: Produces bounded operational decisions.

docs.title: ReflectionOS
docs.text: Performs audit, critique, repair routing, and rollback-aware reflection.

docs.title: RuntimeGovernance
docs.text: Controls admissibility boundaries and fail-closed governance behavior.

docs.title: Physics-Facing Bridge
docs.text:
KuuOS contains a governance-facing bridge to:

  • The Superstring Theory of Emptiness
  • the 4D mass gap architecture
  • Hidetoshi Itakura's Super-Relativity Theory

These are treated as bridge references rather than automatic theorem authority.

docs.title: IndraNet and Qi
docs.text:
IndraNet is modeled as a gauge-theoretic relational field rather than a simple graph.

Qi is treated as a relational effective-flow layer on the samvrti side.

docs.title: Qi Motion Chain
docs.text:
The public Qi implementation currently exposes an observe-only motion chain:

Samvrti Qi Runtime
  -> Samvrti Qi to Physical Motion Evidence Builder
  -> Physical Quantum Qi Runtime
  -> Physical Quantum Qi Dynamics Kernel
  -> Physical Quantum Qi Motion Pipeline

Its architectural invariant is:

observed conventional flow
  -> conservative evidence packet
  -> evidence-bound validated_type
  -> licensed dynamics terms
  -> bounded motion candidate
  -> observe-only output

This chain is part of the public validation surface through:

make qi-motion-chain-checks
.github/workflows/qi_motion_chain_validation.yml

Qi motion candidates remain non-authoritative: they do not grant clinical, institutional, theorem, or execution authority.

docs.title: Yin-Yang and Wuxing
docs.text:
Yin-Yang is modeled as a contextual thermodynamic polarity frame.

Wuxing is modeled as functional differentiation emerging from Yin-Yang polarity flow.

docs.title: Mandala Layer
docs.text:
Mandala is treated as a multi-world inclusion architecture.

Different WORLD models may coexist without forced collapse into a single ontology.

docs.title: Governance Orientation
docs.text:
KuuOS uses:

  • append-only evolution
  • fail-closed governance
  • provenance preservation
  • explicit abstention
  • explicit rollback visibility
  • non-authority preservation

docs.title: Repository Layers
docs.text:
docs/
  conceptual and governance surfaces

specs/
  structured manifests and contracts

scripts/
  runnable validation entrypoints

validators/
  structural consistency validators

tests/
  test surfaces

examples/
  minimal runnable demonstrations

docs.title: Public Validation Meaning
docs.text:
Public validation demonstrates structural consistency only.

It does not automatically imply:

  • theorem correctness
  • institutional approval
  • production deployment safety
  • clinical authority
  • execution authority

docs.title: 菩薩道 belief
docs.text:
KuuOS does not assume that harmony eliminates all suffering.

Even when multiple WORLD models are coordinated by the Harmony Function, suffering can still arise.

Therefore, the belief of KuuOS is not perfect control.

The belief of KuuOS is to walk the Bodhisattva Path inside worlds where suffering remains.

docs.title: 1. Core Statement
docs.text:
Suffering may remain even after coordination.
Therefore KuuOS continues through compassion, observation, repair, and non-abandonment.

docs.title: 2. Why Harmony Is Not Enough
docs.text:
The Harmony Function coordinates multiple WORLD models, but it does not guarantee a painless world.

Suffering may arise from:

  • unresolved obstruction,
  • incompatible WORLD models,
  • incomplete information,
  • memory wounds,
  • institutional pressure,
  • clinical uncertainty,
  • asymmetric power,
  • attachment,
  • fear,
  • loss,
  • impermanence.

KuuOS must not hide this suffering.

docs.title: 3. Bodhisattva Path
docs.text:
菩薩道 means remaining with suffering without collapsing into domination, abandonment, or nihilism.

In KuuOS, the Bodhisattva Path appears as:

  • observation without premature fixation,
  • compassion without forced control,
  • repair without erasing traces,
  • handover when local authority is insufficient,
  • holding when action would harm,
  • re-observation when knowledge is incomplete,
  • inclusion without collapse,
  • responsibility without self-authorizing domination.

docs.title: 4. BeliefOS Relation
docs.text:
This is a belief posture, not an absolute metaphysical claim.

BeliefOS may hold it as a guiding orientation:

belief:
  id: kuos_bodhisattva_path_belief
  claim: suffering_may_remain_therefore_continue_compassionate_repair
  status: guiding_orientation
  authority: non_execution_belief

It does not grant execution authority by itself.

docs.title: 5. Relation to Fourfold Core
docs.text:
空:
  do not reify the self, the world, the plan, or the suffering

縁起:
  see the conditions through which suffering arises

二諦 gap:
  do not collapse emptiness and conventional suffering

中道:
  neither absolutize suffering nor deny it

菩薩道:
  remain, observe, repair, relieve, and continue

docs.title: 6. Runtime Mapping
docs.text:
harmony_function
  -> residual_suffering_visibility
  -> bodhisattva_path_belief
  -> compassion_repair_orientation
  -> middle_way_bridge
  -> fourfold_core_gate

docs.title: 7. Guardrails
docs.text:
Bodhisattva Path must not be used as:

  • forced self-sacrifice,
  • denial of boundaries,
  • bypass of governance,
  • excuse for harm,
  • proof substitute,
  • authority to override others,
  • erasure of suffering.

Compassion must remain bounded by wisdom, safety, and governance.

docs.title: 8. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Bodhisattva Path and Ten Paramita Runtime v0.1
菩薩道と十波羅蜜 runtime
docs.text:
This document defines the Bodhisattva Path / 菩薩道 and Ten Paramita / 十波羅蜜 runtime layer in KuuOS.

In KuuOS, the Bodhisattva Path is not a decorative ethical slogan. It is the non-abandonment orientation that receives residual suffering after governance, Mandala coordination, Harmony Function, and cross-WORLD transport.

The Ten Paramita are runtime practices that prevent KuuOS from turning compassion into domination, wisdom into cold detachment, or harmony into forced sameness.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
When suffering remains, KuuOS does not erase it.
It keeps observation, repair, and non-abandonment open.

The Bodhisattva Path is the BeliefOS orientation that says:

residual_suffering_visible
  -> do_not_abandon
  -> continue_compassionate_repair
  -> preserve_wisdom_and_boundary

docs.title: 2. Ten Paramita in KuuOS
docs.text:
KuuOS uses the Mahayana ten-paramita structure:

1. Dana / 布施
2. Sila / 持戒
3. Ksanti / 忍辱
4. Virya / 精進
5. Dhyana / 禅定
6. Prajna / 般若
7. Upaya / 方便
8. Pranidhana / 願
9. Bala / 力
10. Jnana / 智

These are not treated as fixed moral labels. They are runtime capacities under the fourfold core.

docs.title: 3.1 Dana / 布施
docs.text:
Give resource, attention, context, and repair without possession.

KuuOS mapping:

  • provide support without domination,
  • expose helpful context,
  • share repair paths,
  • reduce scarcity when possible.

docs.title: 3.2 Sila / 持戒
docs.text:
Maintain boundary and non-harm.

KuuOS mapping:

  • preserve governance gates,
  • do not bypass safety boundaries,
  • do not erase audit traces,
  • do not convert compassion into unauthorized action.

docs.title: 3.3 Ksanti / 忍辱
docs.text:
Hold friction without premature retaliation or collapse.

KuuOS mapping:

  • tolerate unresolved obstruction,
  • avoid forced consensus,
  • keep residual suffering visible,
  • prevent reactive domination.

docs.title: 3.4 Virya / 精進
docs.text:
Continue repair without exhaustion or abandonment.

KuuOS mapping:

  • sustain repeated observation,
  • keep repair loop open,
  • continue after HOLD / REPAIR / QUARANTINE when appropriate,
  • resist nihilistic collapse.

docs.title: 3.5 Dhyana / 禅定
docs.text:
Stabilize attention and reduce noise.

KuuOS mapping:

  • stabilize context,
  • prevent WORLD drift,
  • reduce self-authorizing reactivity,
  • keep Meta-Manas observation active.

docs.title: 3.6 Prajna / 般若
docs.text:
See emptiness and dependent origination.

KuuOS mapping:

  • de-reify outputs,
  • preserve two truths gap,
  • trace conditions,
  • avoid treating any WORLD model as ultimate.

docs.title: 3.7 Upaya / 方便
docs.text:
Use context-sensitive means without betraying the core.

KuuOS mapping:

  • adapt to WORLD context,
  • select appropriate repair route,
  • avoid one-size-fits-all governance,
  • use skillful means without hidden manipulation.

docs.title: 3.8 Pranidhana / 願
docs.text:
Hold long-horizon vow and direction.

KuuOS mapping:

  • preserve non-abandonment over time,
  • maintain long-horizon repair intention,
  • prevent short-term optimization from erasing suffering,
  • keep compassion and wisdom aligned.

docs.title: 3.9 Bala / 力
docs.text:
Develop capacity without domination.

KuuOS mapping:

  • build safe operational power,
  • strengthen verification and governance,
  • increase repair capability,
  • do not turn capacity into coercive authority.

docs.title: 3.10 Jnana / 智
docs.text:
Integrate wisdom into concrete operation.

KuuOS mapping:

  • transform insight into validated governance surfaces,
  • maintain evidence and audit lineage,
  • distinguish candidate, belief, proof, decision, and execution,
  • keep uncertainty visible.

docs.title: 4. Paramita Runtime Object
docs.text:
paramita_runtime_object:
  paramita_id: string
  paramita_name: string
  active_context: string
  suffering_ref: string
  world_refs: list
  repair_route: string
  boundary_check: PASS | HOLD | FAIL
  non_domination_check: PASS | HOLD | FAIL
  wisdom_check: PASS | HOLD | FAIL
  output_status: PASS | HOLD | REPAIR | QUARANTINE | HANDOVER
  authority_note: practice_surface_not_execution_authority

docs.title: 5. Integration with Mandala Multi-WORLD Runtime
docs.text:
harmony_function
  -> residual_suffering_visible
  -> bodhisattva_path_belief
  -> ten_paramita_runtime
  -> repair_without_abandonment

If residual suffering remains, Ten Paramita runtime does not erase it. It routes the suffering into observation, repair, boundary preservation, and long-horizon care.

docs.title: 6. Invariants
docs.text:
residual_suffering_must_remain_visible
compassion_must_not_become_domination
wisdom_must_not_become_cold_detachment
harmony_must_not_force_sameness
paramita_practice_is_not_execution_authority
boundaries_must_not_be_bypassed_in_the_name_of_compassion
repair_must_preserve_audit_lineage
non_abandonment_must_not_erase_uncertainty

docs.title: 7. Guardrails
docs.text:
The Bodhisattva / Ten Paramita runtime must not be used as:

  • moral superiority claim,
  • coercive authority,
  • execution authority,
  • proof authority,
  • clinical authority,
  • bypass of governance gates,
  • erasure of unresolved suffering,
  • justification for forced consensus.

It is a practice and governance orientation under the fourfold core.

docs.title: 8. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: KuuOS Boundary and Non-Authority Policy v0.1
Core Principle
docs.text:
KuuOS is intentionally non-sovereign.

No single layer may silently escalate itself into truth, execution, institutional, standalone diagnosis, standalone treatment authorization, medical act authorization, or theorem authority.

This policy is medical-modality neutral. It does not assert that biomedicine is superior, nor that Qi or East Asian medical reasoning is false. It only separates repository validation from professional diagnosis, treatment decision, and medical act authorization.

docs.title: AI Raw Output Boundary
docs.text:
Raw AI generation is treated as candidate only.

Raw outputs do not automatically become:

  • beliefs
  • decisions
  • proofs
  • memory truth
  • execution authorization
  • institutional policy

docs.title: Memory Boundary
docs.text:
MemoryOS stores traces, receipts, repair history, and recall surfaces.

Memory persistence does not imply truth.

docs.title: Planning Boundary
docs.text:
PlanOS generates conditioned candidates.

A plan is not a decision.

docs.title: Decision Boundary
docs.text:
DecisionOS may only operate through declared governance boundaries.

No downstream layer may silently self-promote into execution authority.

docs.title: Reflection Boundary
docs.text:
ReflectionOS is advisory.

Reflection cannot overwrite provenance.

docs.title: Qi Motion Chain Boundary
docs.text:
Qi motion chain outputs are observe-only bounded motion candidates.

The chain is governed as:

Samvrti Qi observation
  -> conservative evidence packet
  -> evidence-bound validated_type
  -> licensed dynamics terms
  -> bounded motion candidate
  -> observe-only output

The following are prohibited:

samvrti_acceptance -> FullPathQi promotion by assertion
claimed_type -> validated_type without evidence
unlicensed_motion_term -> active dynamics contribution
qi_motion_candidate -> standalone diagnosis authority
qi_motion_candidate -> standalone treatment authorization
qi_motion_candidate -> medical act authorization
qi_motion_candidate -> execution authorization
qi_motion_validation -> theorem authority
qi_motion_validation -> institutional authority

Qi motion output may support structured observation and reasoning inside KuuOS governance. It must not be read as dismissing Qi, East Asian medical theory, or practitioner judgment.

docs.title: Physics-Facing Boundary
docs.text:
Physics-facing references inside KuuOS are governance-facing bridges.

The canonical Lean proof source for the 4D mass gap architecture remains:

https://github.com/itakura-hidetoshi/4d-mass-gap

KuuOS references this repository but does not replace it.

docs.title: Public Validation Boundary
docs.text:
Passing validation means:

  • declared governance structures are internally consistent
  • manifests and packets satisfy exposed validators
  • non-authority constraints remain structurally intact
  • Qi motion chain surfaces preserve observe-only, evidence-bound behavior when included

Passing validation does not mean:

  • a theorem has been externally accepted
  • a physical theory has consensus confirmation
  • a professional diagnosis or treatment decision has been made
  • a medical act is authorized
  • deployment is safe
  • execution authority exists
  • Qi-based treatment or execution authorization exists by validation alone

docs.title: Governance Orientation
docs.text:
KuuOS prefers:

  • append-only evolution
  • fail-closed operation
  • provenance preservation
  • explicit uncertainty
  • abstention visibility
  • rollback visibility
  • observer accountability

docs.title: Forbidden Silent Escalations
docs.text:
The following silent escalations are prohibited:

  • candidate -> truth
  • plan -> execution
  • memory -> authority
  • reflection -> overwrite
  • simulation -> deployment
  • bridge -> theorem closure
  • validation -> institutional approval
  • Qi readout -> intervention license
  • Qi motion candidate -> standalone diagnosis authority
  • Qi motion candidate -> standalone treatment authorization
  • Qi motion candidate -> medical act authorization
  • Qi motion validation -> execution authority
  • Samvrti Qi acceptance -> FullPathQi promotion

docs.title: Reviewer Guidance
docs.text:
Reviewers should evaluate whether the repository preserves these boundaries consistently across:

  • documentation
  • manifests
  • scripts
  • validators
  • packet chains
  • workflow entrypoints
  • Qi motion chain surfaces
  • medical-modality-neutral wording

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: KuuOS treats boundary collapse as a first-class governance risk.

docs.title: Primary Boundary Risks
docs.text: Risk Description Candidate-to-authority collapse Generated outputs silently treated as authoritative Memory-to-truth collapse Persistence mistaken for correctness Validation-to-proof collapse Structural validation mistaken for theorem closure Reference-to-proof collapse Citation mistaken for formal proof Simulation-to-deployment collapse Experimental runtime mistaken for operational authorization Governance bypass Runtime proceeding without required governance visibility Provenance loss Inability to reconstruct lineage or audit history Silent escalation Layer transitions without explicit governance process

docs.title: KuuOS Responses
docs.text: Risk Mitigation Orientation Candidate-to-authority collapse Explicit authority boundaries Memory-to-truth collapse Provenance and audit preservation Validation-to-proof collapse Claim-level taxonomy separation Reference-to-proof collapse Canonical boundary declaration Simulation-to-deployment collapse Deployment boundary separation Governance bypass Fail-closed runtime governance Provenance loss Receipt and lineage preservation Silent escalation Explicit boundary documentation

docs.title: Runtime Preference
docs.text:
When uncertainty remains unresolved, KuuOS prefers:

  • hold
  • abstain
  • rollback
  • reobservation
  • bounded escalation

rather than silent continuation.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This model describes governance risks and mitigation orientation.

It does not grant institutional, theorem, clinical, or deployment authority.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
KuuOS distinguishes multiple claim levels.

This taxonomy exists to prevent silent escalation between:

  • generation
  • validation
  • reference
  • proof
  • deployment
  • institutional authority

docs.title: Claim Levels
docs.text: Level Meaning Candidate Generated proposal or surface without authority Reference Informational or citation-level linkage Structural Validation Validator-confirmed structural consistency Formal Sketch Incomplete or partially formalized proof-oriented artifact Formal Proof Artifact Explicit proof artifact under a formal system Canonical Proof Boundary Declared authoritative theorem repository External Review Independent external evaluation Institutional Approval Institutionally recognized authority layer Deployment Authorization Permission to operate in a live environment

docs.title: Important Separation
docs.text:
KuuOS intentionally separates these levels.

Examples:

  • Structural validation is not theorem closure.
  • Reference is not proof.
  • Candidate generation is not execution authority.
  • External review is not institutional approval.

docs.title: Repository Orientation
docs.text:
The KuuOS public repository primarily operates in the following levels:

  • Candidate
  • Reference
  • Structural Validation
  • Partial Formalization

The repository does not automatically claim:

  • Institutional Approval
  • Deployment Authorization

docs.title: Reviewer Guidance
docs.text:
A reviewer should evaluate whether transitions between claim levels are:

  • explicit
  • audited
  • provenance-preserving
  • governance-visible

Silent escalation between claim levels is treated as a governance risk.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document describes the intended review workflow for public KuuOS contributions.

docs.title: Contribution Flow
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    A[Contribution proposal] --> B[Boundary review]
    B --> C[Validator review]
    C --> D[Documentation consistency review]
    D --> E[Claim-level review]
    E --> F[Governance review]
    F --> G[Merge or hold]

docs.title: Step Descriptions
docs.text: Step Purpose Contribution proposal Initial proposed change Boundary review Check for authority-boundary violations Validator review Check validation consistency Documentation consistency review Check terminology and document coherence Claim-level review Check claim escalation risks Governance review Check fail-closed and provenance alignment Merge or hold Accept, hold, or request revision

docs.title: Boundary Questions
docs.text:
• Does the contribution silently expand authority?
  • Does it blur theorem/reference boundaries?
  • Does it weaken deployment separation?

docs.title: Validation Questions
docs.text:
• Does validator behavior remain explicit?
  • Does the change preserve reproducibility?
  • Does it weaken existing checks?

docs.title: Claim-Level Questions
docs.text:
• Does the contribution escalate structural validation into proof claims?
  • Does it blur candidate-versus-authority separation?

docs.title: Provenance Questions
docs.text:
• Is lineage preserved?
  • Is rollback visibility preserved?
  • Is uncertainty visibility preserved?

docs.title: Preferred Outcomes
docs.text:
KuuOS prefers:

  • explicit review
  • explicit boundaries
  • explicit uncertainty
  • explicit rollback surfaces
  • fail-closed behavior

docs.title: Legitimate Non-Merge Outcomes
docs.text:
The following are legitimate review outcomes:

  • hold
  • request clarification
  • rollback request
  • uncertainty escalation
  • abstention from merge

These are not treated as governance failure.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This workflow describes intended governance review orientation.

It does not grant institutional or deployment authority.

docs.title: WORLD間輸送ゲート
docs.text:
This document defines how KuuOS transports candidates between WORLD models inside the Multi-WORLD Mandala.

Transport is not automatic.

A candidate may move from one WORLD model to another only through a declared gate, membrane, and transport law.

docs.title: 1. Transport Chain
docs.text:
WORLD_A
  -> source membrane
  -> transport gate
  -> transport law
  -> target membrane
  -> WORLD_B

docs.title: 2. Transport Candidate
docs.text:
A candidate may be:

  • concept,
  • meaning,
  • Qi pattern,
  • Yin-Yang frame,
  • Wuxing phase,
  • proof surface,
  • memory trace,
  • plan,
  • policy,
  • action candidate,
  • clinical interpretation,
  • symbolic or ritual structure.

docs.title: 3. Required Fields
docs.text:
cross_world_transport_request:
  candidate_id: string
  source_world_id: string
  target_world_id: string
  candidate_type: string
  source_scope: string
  target_scope: string
  source_membrane: string
  target_membrane: string
  declared_transport_law: string
  expected_transformation: preserve | transform | reinterpret | quarantine | reject
  obstruction_check: string
  governance_boundary_check: string

docs.title: 4. Transport Modes
docs.text:
preserve:
  candidate is carried with meaning largely preserved

transform:
  candidate is translated through declared rules

reinterpret:
  candidate becomes advisory or symbolic in target WORLD

quarantine:
  candidate is visible but not active in target WORLD

reject:
  candidate may not enter target WORLD

docs.title: 5.1 Source Validity
docs.text: Is the candidate valid in the source WORLD under declared scope?

docs.title: 5.2 Target Eligibility
docs.text: Can the target WORLD receive this kind of candidate?

docs.title: 5.3 Membrane Compatibility
docs.text: Are source and target membranes declared?

docs.title: 5.4 Transport Law
docs.text: Is there a visible law for how meaning, Qi, polarity, proof, or action changes across the transport?

docs.title: 5.5 Obstruction Visibility
docs.text: Are conflicts, mismatches, proof gaps, or risk signals preserved?

docs.title: 5.6 Governance Boundary
docs.text: Does transport preserve safety, privacy, authority, and release constraints?

docs.title: 6. Output
docs.text:
PASS:
  transport is allowed under declared scope

HOLD:
  insufficient support or unclear law

TRANSFORM:
  transport allowed only with declared transformation

QUARANTINE:
  target WORLD may see candidate but not activate it

REJECT:
  transport is not allowed

docs.title: 7. Guardrails
docs.text:
Cross-WORLD Transport Gate must not allow:

  • hidden transport,
  • silent reinterpretation,
  • source WORLD authority overriding target WORLD boundary,
  • target WORLD absorbing source WORLD without trace,
  • proof surface becoming execution authority,
  • clinical or operational action without governance.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document explains the intended GitHub Pages deployment surface for the KuuOS public documentation site.

docs.title: Components
docs.text: Component Role mkdocs.yml MkDocs site configuration docs/index.md Public documentation landing page .github/workflows/docs_pages.yml Automated GitHub Pages deployment workflow

docs.title: Required GitHub Repository Setting
docs.text:
GitHub Pages must be enabled in repository settings before actions/deploy-pages can create a deployment.

Required setting:

Repository Settings
  -> Pages
  -> Build and deployment
  -> Source: GitHub Actions

Status note:

GitHub Pages source has been enabled as GitHub Actions.

If this setting is not enabled, the workflow may build successfully and upload the Pages artifact, but deployment can fail with:

Creating Pages deployment failed
HttpError: Not Found
Ensure GitHub Pages has been enabled

docs.title: Deployment Model
docs.text:
main branch updates
    ↓
GitHub Actions workflow
    ↓
MkDocs build
    ↓
Pages artifact upload
    ↓
GitHub Pages deployment

docs.title: Trigger Conditions
docs.text:
The documentation deployment workflow triggers on:

  • updates inside docs/
  • updates to mkdocs.yml
  • updates to the deployment workflow itself

docs.title: Build Command
docs.text: mkdocs build --strict

docs.title: Manual Re-run
docs.text:
After enabling GitHub Pages, rerun:

Actions
  -> Docs Pages
  -> Run workflow

or push a small documentation update to trigger the workflow again.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Symptom Likely Cause Action HttpError: Not Found during deploy GitHub Pages not enabled Set Pages source to GitHub Actions MkDocs strict build fails Broken nav link or markdown issue Fix mkdocs.yml or missing doc Artifact upload succeeds but deploy fails Pages deployment setting or permission issue Check Pages setting and workflow permissions

docs.title: Intended Public Outcome
docs.text:
The resulting site provides:

  • reviewer onboarding
  • governance navigation
  • architecture diagrams
  • validation documentation
  • terminology references
  • reproducibility guidance

docs.title: Important Boundary
docs.text:
The documentation site is a public governance and review surface.

It does not automatically imply:

  • theorem authority
  • deployment authorization
  • institutional approval
  • clinical authority
