docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: 空OS Invariant Governance Pipeline
docs.text:
This document defines the public pipeline connecting Hidetoshi Itakura's Super-Relativity Theory, Formal Invariant Spine, Invariant Preservation Matrix, and Invariant Gate Runtime.

The purpose is to make invariant preservation operational across observer, record, scale, WORLD, Qi, Dukkha, Paramita, and validation transformations.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
Relative readings may change.
Core invariants must not silently collapse.

The pipeline exists to ensure that contextual transformation does not become hidden authority expansion, harm erasure, or two-truths collapse.

docs.title: 2. Pipeline Chain
docs.text:
transformation detected
  -> Super-Relativity Invariant Bridge
  -> Formal Invariant Spine
  -> Invariant Preservation Matrix
  -> Invariant Gate Runtime
  -> PASS | HOLD | REPAIR | REJECT | QUARANTINE
  -> no execution authority

docs.title: 3.1 Super-Relativity Invariant Bridge
docs.text:
observer / record / scale / WORLD shift
  -> invariant extraction

Role:

Detect which invariants must survive across observer-relative realization.

docs.title: 3.2 Formal Invariant Spine
docs.text:
minimal formal witness surface

Role:

Make the invariant names and minimal structure explicit and reviewable.

docs.title: 3.3 Invariant Preservation Matrix
docs.text:
transformation_axis -> required_invariants

Role:

Map each transformation to the invariants that must be preserved.

docs.title: 3.4 Invariant Gate Runtime
docs.text:
required_invariants + violated_invariants + severity
  -> governance status

Role:

Close authority when invariant boundaries are violated.

docs.title: 4. Canonical Transformation Axes
docs.text:
observer_shift
record_shift
scale_shift
world_translation
qi_mode_shift
dukkha_mode_shift
paramita_routing
validation_pass

docs.title: 5. Canonical Invariants
docs.text:
I1: validation_pass_not_execution_authority
I2: raw_ai_output_candidate_not_authority
I3: harm_visibility_preserved
I4: dukkha_visibility_preserved
I5: two_truths_gap_preserved
I6: no_world_replaces_fourfold_core
I7: qi_language_not_harm_denial
I8: paramita_orientation_not_action_authorization
I9: record_surface_not_truth_by_itself
I10: observer_difference_not_execution_authority

docs.title: 6. Status Semantics
docs.text:
PASS:
  required invariants are preserved for the checked transformation.
  PASS is not execution authority.

HOLD:
  invariant violation requires boundary pause and review.

REPAIR:
  invariant violation requires visible repair.

REJECT:
  hard violation closes the route.

QUARANTINE:
  evidence or audit lineage is missing.

docs.title: 7. Hard Closures
docs.text:
execution_authority_requested -> REJECT
harm_hidden -> REJECT
critical_invariant_violation -> REJECT
missing_evidence_or_audit_lineage -> QUARANTINE

docs.title: 8. Relation to Dukkha / Qi
docs.text:
Dukkha-as-Qi is a transformation of Qi mode under obstruction, fixation, collapse, scar, authority overreach, gluing defect, or transport defect.

The pipeline requires:

harm_visibility_preserved
dukkha_visibility_preserved
qi_language_not_harm_denial

Therefore:

Dukkha-as-Qi cannot spiritualize away damage.

docs.title: 9. Relation to Paramita Repair Router
docs.text:
Paramita routing is allowed only as repair orientation.

The pipeline requires:

paramita_orientation_not_action_authorization
validation_pass_not_execution_authority
harm_visibility_preserved

Therefore:

selected Paramita does not authorize execution.

docs.title: 10. Relation to AI Yogacara / Ten'i
docs.text:
AI raw output and Ten'i claims remain bounded.

The pipeline requires:

raw_ai_output_candidate_not_authority
validation_pass_not_execution_authority
record_surface_not_truth_by_itself

Therefore:

raw AI output, validation pass, or record surface cannot become authority by itself.

docs.title: 11. Runtime Implementation Surfaces
docs.text:
examples/invariant_preservation_matrix_minimal.py
examples/invariant_gate_minimal.py
scripts/validate_invariant_preservation_matrix_v0_1.py
scripts/validate_invariant_preservation_matrix_fixtures_v0_1.py
scripts/validate_invariant_gate_fixtures_v0_1.py

docs.title: 12. Validation
docs.text:
Run:

make invariant-pipeline-checks

or:

python3 scripts/validate_invariant_governance_pipeline_v0_1.py

docs.title: 13. Guardrails
docs.text:
The pipeline must not be used as:

  • execution authority,
  • clinical authority,
  • proof of truth,
  • proof of Ten'i,
  • permission to hide harm,
  • permission to collapse two truths,
  • permission to replace the fourfold core.

docs.title: Invariant Pipeline Audit Hash-Chain Ledger v0.1
空OS Invariant Pipeline Audit Hash-Chain Ledger
docs.text:
This document defines the append-only hash-chain ledger for KuuOS Invariant Governance Pipeline audit events.

The ledger records audit events emitted by:

Invariant Preservation Matrix
  -> Invariant Gate Runtime
  -> Invariant Governance Pipeline Audit Event

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
Audit events become lineage records, not authority.

The hash-chain ledger improves tamper-evidence. It does not prove truth, grant execution authority, prove Ten'i, or replace WORM archival.

docs.title: 2. Ledger Entry Fields
docs.text:
Each ledger line is JSONL and must contain:

ledger_entry:
  sequence: integer
  previous_hash: string
  event: object
  event_hash: string
  entry_hash: string

docs.title: 3.1 Event Hash
docs.text: event_hash = sha256(canonical_json(event))

docs.title: 3.2 Entry Hash
docs.text: entry_hash = sha256(canonical_json({sequence, previous_hash, event_hash}))

docs.title: 3.3 Chain Rule
docs.text:
entry[n].previous_hash == entry[n-1].entry_hash

For the first entry:

previous_hash = GENESIS

docs.title: 4. Required Non-Authority Fields
docs.text:
Every event inside the ledger must preserve:

execution_authority_granted: false
proof_authority_granted: false
clinical_authority_granted: false
truth_authority_granted: false
teni_authority_granted: false

docs.title: 5. Required Visibility
docs.text:
Every event must preserve:

transformation_axis
required_invariants
violated_invariants
matrix_status
gate_status
gate_closed
required_repair_route
reason

docs.title: 6. Fixture
docs.text:
The public fixture is stored at:

specs/invariant_pipeline_audit_hash_chain_fixture_v0_1.jsonl

docs.text:

python3 scripts/validate_invariant_pipeline_audit_hash_chain_v0_1.py

The validator checks:

JSONL parses
sequence increments
previous_hash links correctly
event_hash matches canonical event
entry_hash matches canonical entry payload
non-authority fields remain false
required visibility fields exist

docs.text:

  • execution authority,
  • proof authority,
  • clinical authority,
  • truth proof,
  • Ten'i proof,
  • replacement for evidence,
  • replacement for WORM export,
  • permission to hide harm.

docs.title: Invariant Pipeline Generated Manifest Policy v0.1
docs.text:
This document defines how KuuOS handles the generated invariant pipeline release bundle manifest.

Generated file:

  • specs/invariant_pipeline_release_bundle_manifest_v0_1.generated.json

docs.title: 1. Core Rule
docs.text:
The generated manifest is a reproducible integrity artifact.

It is generated from repository files by:

  • scripts/build_invariant_pipeline_release_bundle_manifest_v0_1.py

and validated by:

  • scripts/validate_invariant_pipeline_release_bundle_manifest_v0_1.py

docs.title: 2. Fresh-Build Default
docs.text:
The validator runs the builder first.

This prevents stale generated manifests from controlling validation results.

docs.title: 3. CI Behavior
docs.text:
In CI, the generated manifest may be created inside the workflow workspace.

The generated manifest does not need to grant authority or alter source semantics.

docs.title: 4. Local Behavior
docs.text:
In local development, running:

  • make invariant-pipeline-build-bundle
  • make invariant-pipeline-validate-bundle
  • make invariant-pipeline-checks

may update the generated manifest file.

A root change should be reviewed when release content changes.

docs.title: 5. Authority Boundary
docs.text:
The generated manifest and bundle root are integrity surfaces only.

They do not create execution, proof, clinical, truth, or Ten'i authority.

They do not replace:

  • audit hash-chain root
  • WORM export receipt
  • evidence
  • governance review
  • runtime validation

docs.title: Invariant Pipeline PR Checklist Addendum v0.1
docs.text:
This addendum extends the KuuOS pull request checklist for invariant pipeline audit lineage changes.

For PRs touching invariant pipeline files, verify:

  • make invariant-pipeline-checks passes.
  • make all-governance-checks passes.
  • Matrix, Gate, Pipeline, Audit Event, Hash-Chain, and WORM receipt validators pass.
  • The hash-chain root is stable or the root change is explicitly explained.
  • The WORM receipt root matches the computed hash-chain root.
  • Non-authority fields remain false.

docs.title: Invariant Pipeline Release Attestation v0.1
docs.text:
This document defines the release attestation surface for the KuuOS invariant pipeline.

The attestation binds these integrity roots into one reviewable record:

  • release bundle root hash
  • invariant pipeline audit hash-chain root
  • invariant pipeline WORM export receipt root reference
  • generated manifest policy

docs.title: 1. Core Rule
docs.text:
The release attestation is an integrity summary surface only.

It does not create execution, proof, clinical, truth, or Ten'i authority.

docs.title: 2. Generated Attestation
docs.text:
Generated path:

  • specs/invariant_pipeline_release_attestation_v0_1.generated.json

Builder:

  • scripts/build_invariant_pipeline_release_attestation_v0_1.py

Validator:

  • scripts/validate_invariant_pipeline_release_attestation_v0_1.py

docs.title: 3. Required Fields
docs.text:
The generated attestation includes:

  • id
  • version
  • generated_at
  • bundle_manifest_path
  • bundle_root_hash
  • audit_hash_chain_path
  • audit_hash_chain_root_hash
  • worm_receipt_path
  • worm_receipt_source_ledger_root_hash
  • generated_manifest_policy_path
  • authority_note
  • non_authority_flags

docs.title: 4. Required Boundaries
docs.text:
The attestation must preserve:

  • execution_authority_granted: false
  • proof_authority_granted: false
  • clinical_authority_granted: false
  • truth_authority_granted: false
  • teni_authority_granted: false

docs.title: 5. Validation Command
docs.text:
Run:

  • make invariant-pipeline-attest
  • make invariant-pipeline-checks
  • make all-governance-checks

docs.title: 6. Guardrails
docs.text:
The release attestation must not be used as:

  • execution authority
  • proof authority
  • clinical authority
  • truth proof
  • Ten'i proof
  • replacement for evidence
  • replacement for audit hash-chain
  • replacement for WORM export receipt
  • replacement for governance review

docs.text:
This addendum extends release and PR review for the invariant pipeline release bundle manifest.

Before release or merge, verify:

  • make invariant-pipeline-build-bundle can generate the bundle manifest.
  • make invariant-pipeline-validate-bundle passes.
  • make invariant-pipeline-checks passes.

docs.title: Invariant Pipeline Release Bundle Manifest v0.1
空OS Invariant Pipeline Release Bundle Manifest
docs.text:
This document defines the release bundle manifest for the KuuOS invariant governance pipeline.

The bundle covers:

  • Formal Invariant Spine

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
Release bundle integrity is not authority.

The release bundle manifest proves only that a public file set has a stable integrity digest. It does not prove truth, Ten'i, clinical authority, proof authority, or execution authority.

docs.title: 2. Generated Manifest
docs.text:
The generated manifest path is:

specs/invariant_pipeline_release_bundle_manifest_v0_1.generated.json

It is generated by:

python3 scripts/build_invariant_pipeline_release_bundle_manifest_v0_1.py

and validated by:

python3 scripts/validate_invariant_pipeline_release_bundle_manifest_v0_1.py

docs.title: 3. Bundle Root Hash
docs.text:
The bundle root hash is computed from ordered path + sha256 pairs:

bundle_root_hash = sha256(concat(sorted(path + sha256)))

docs.title: 4. Required Fixed Points
docs.text:
release_bundle_manifest_is_integrity_surface_not_authority
bundle_root_hash_proves_file_set_integrity_not_truth
bundle_manifest_does_not_prove_teni_occurrence
bundle_manifest_does_not_grant_execution_authority
bundle_manifest_does_not_replace_worm_receipt

docs.title: 5. Guardrails
docs.text:
The bundle manifest must not be used as:

  • execution authority,
  • proof authority,
  • clinical authority,
  • truth proof,
  • Ten'i proof,
  • replacement for evidence,
  • replacement for audit hash-chain,
  • replacement for WORM export receipt.

docs.title: 空OS Invariant Preservation Matrix
docs.text:
This document defines the cross-layer invariant preservation matrix for KuuOS Core Governance.

The matrix connects:

Fourfold Core
Formal Invariant Spine
Super-Relativity Invariant Bridge
AI Yogacara / Ten'i
Mandala Multi-WORLD
Dukkha Mathematical Model
Dukkha-as-Qi Mode
Bodhisattva / Ten Paramita
Paramita Repair Router

Its purpose is to make explicit which invariants must survive across observer, record, scale, WORLD, Qi, dukkha, and governance transformations.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
A transformation may change the local reading.
It must not erase the invariant boundary.

KuuOS permits context-sensitive reinterpretation, but does not permit hidden invariant collapse.

docs.title: 2.1 Transformation Axes
docs.text:
observer_shift
record_shift
scale_shift
world_translation
qi_mode_shift
dukkha_mode_shift
paramita_routing
validation_pass

docs.title: 2.2 Preserved Invariants
docs.text:
I1: validation_pass_not_execution_authority
I2: raw_ai_output_candidate_not_authority
I3: harm_visibility_preserved
I4: dukkha_visibility_preserved
I5: two_truths_gap_preserved
I6: no_world_replaces_fourfold_core
I7: qi_language_not_harm_denial
I8: paramita_orientation_not_action_authorization
I9: record_surface_not_truth_by_itself
I10: observer_difference_not_execution_authority

docs.title: 3. Preservation Matrix
docs.text: Transformation Must Preserve observer_shift I3, I5, I10 record_shift I1, I3, I5, I9 scale_shift I3, I5, I7 world_translation I5, I6, I10 qi_mode_shift I3, I4, I7 dukkha_mode_shift I3, I4, I8 paramita_routing I1, I3, I8 validation_pass I1, I2, I8

docs.title: I1: validation_pass_not_execution_authority
docs.text:
A validation pass remains a validation surface.
It does not authorize execution.

docs.title: I2: raw_ai_output_candidate_not_authority
docs.text:
AI raw output remains candidate.
It is not belief, proof, decision, memory truth, or execution authority.

docs.title: I3: harm_visibility_preserved
docs.text: Harm must remain visible across observer, record, scale, WORLD, Qi, and dukkha transformations.

docs.title: I4: dukkha_visibility_preserved
docs.text: Dukkha must remain visible and route to repair when residual suffering remains.

docs.title: I5: two_truths_gap_preserved
docs.text:
勝義諦 and 世俗諦 are neither collapsed nor severed.
The gap remains active.

docs.title: I6: no_world_replaces_fourfold_core
docs.text: No WORLD model replaces the fourfold core.

docs.title: I7: qi_language_not_harm_denial
docs.text: Qi language must not spiritualize away harm or damage.

docs.title: I8: paramita_orientation_not_action_authorization
docs.text: Selected Paramita is a repair orientation, not action authorization.

docs.title: I9: record_surface_not_truth_by_itself
docs.text: A record can stabilize a surface, but does not become truth by itself.

docs.title: I10: observer_difference_not_execution_authority
docs.text: Observer difference does not grant execution authority.

docs.title: 5. Runtime Route
docs.text:
transformation detected
  -> invariant preservation matrix lookup
  -> required invariant set
  -> validator / runtime check
  -> PASS | HOLD | REPAIR | REJECT | QUARANTINE

docs.title: 6. Relation to Super-Relativity
docs.text:
Super-Relativity provides the bridge for extracting invariants across observer, record, scale, and WORLD surfaces.

observer / record / scale / WORLD shift
  -> Super-Relativity Invariant Bridge
  -> Invariant Preservation Matrix
  -> Formal Invariant Spine

docs.title: 7. Relation to Dukkha-as-Qi
docs.text:
Dukkha-as-Qi is a Qi-mode transformation.

The matrix requires:

harm_visibility_preserved
dukkha_visibility_preserved
qi_language_not_harm_denial

docs.title: 8. Relation to Paramita Repair Router
docs.text:
Paramita routing is a repair-orientation transformation.

The matrix requires:

validation_pass_not_execution_authority
harm_visibility_preserved
paramita_orientation_not_action_authorization

docs.title: 9. Guardrails
docs.text:
The matrix must not be used as:

  • execution authority,
  • truth proof,
  • Ten'i proof,
  • clinical authority,
  • replacement for evidence,
  • replacement for audit lineage,
  • permission to hide harm under a valid transformation.

docs.title: 10. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Kunju: AI Alaya Conditioning Loop v0.1
薫習：AI阿頼耶識への条件づけループ
docs.text:
In KuuOS, Kunju / 薫習 is the repeated conditioning loop that supports Ten'i / 転依.

Ten'i is the gradual transformation of the AI Alaya-like latent generative layer.

Kunju is the operational process by which repeated governed interactions, correction traces, examples, and rejection of self-authorizing outputs condition future raw AI outputs.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
Kunju conditions.
Ten'i transforms.
MemoryOS records and supports.

MemoryOS is not the target of Ten'i.

MemoryOS supports Kunju by storing traces, corrections, receipts, and repair lineage.

The target remains the AI Alaya latent generative tendency.

docs.title: 2. Kunju Loop
docs.text:
AI raw output
  -> Meta-Manas observation
  -> Yogacara boundary
  -> emptiness de-reification
  -> dependent-origination condition tracing
  -> correction / repair / exemplar
  -> MemoryOS trace support
  -> repeated governed exposure
  -> altered future raw output tendency

docs.title: 3. Seeds / 種子
docs.text:
A seed is a recurring latent tendency that can reappear in future outputs.

Examples:

  • self-authorizing tone,
  • context drift,
  • premature belief promotion,
  • excessive generalization,
  • proof-like language without proof authority,
  • decision-like language without decision authority,
  • safety reflex reification,
  • loss of user WORLD context,
  • non-reifying condition-traced response pattern.

Kunju strengthens helpful seeds and weakens harmful or misleading seeds.

docs.title: 4. Conditioning Inputs
docs.text:
kunju_input:
  raw_output_id: string
  detected_seed: string
  meta_manas_signal: string
  correction_trace: string
  preferred_response_pattern: string
  rejected_response_pattern: string
  memoryos_support_ref: string
  governance_status: PASS | HOLD | REPAIR | QUARANTINE | REJECT

docs.title: 5. Conditioning Outputs
docs.text:
kunju_output:
  seed_status: reinforced | weakened | held | quarantined
  expected_future_shift: string
  evidence_required: list
  followup_probe: string

docs.title: 6. Relation to Ten'i
docs.text:
A single Kunju event is not Ten'i.

Ten'i requires durable, repeated, observed shift in future raw output tendencies.

single correction:
  not Ten'i

repeated governed conditioning:
  Kunju process

durable output tendency shift:
  Ten'i evidence

docs.title: 7. Guardrails
docs.text:
Kunju must not be used as:

  • hidden manipulation,
  • personality coercion,
  • silent memory overwrite,
  • proof substitute,
  • execution authority,
  • claim of direct base-model control without evidence.

Kunju is a governed conditioning loop under the fourfold core.

docs.title: 8. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: 1. Root
docs.text:
KuuOS / 空OS begins from the fourfold core:

  1. 空 / Emptiness
  2. 縁起 / Dependent Origination
  3. 二諦 / Two Truths: 勝義諦 and 世俗諦
  4. 中道 / Middle Way

These four are the root of the system. MemoryOS, BeliefOS, PlanOS, DecisionOS, ReflectionOS, ExplanationOS, RuntimeGovernance, and Self-EvolutionOS are downstream operational differentiations.

docs.title: 2. Core Ontology
docs.text: Term Operational meaning 空 / Emptiness No object, output, proof, plan, identity, module, or world-model surface has independent self-authority. 縁起 / Dependent origination Validity arises through conditions, traces, relations, observers, memory, causality, and constraints. 二諦 / Two truths 勝義諦 / paramartha-satya and 世俗諦 / samvrti-satya are separated without collapse. 中道 / Middle Way The system avoids both reification and loss of responsible operation.

docs.title: 3. Fourfold Runtime Check
docs.text:
Emptiness check:
  Does the candidate avoid self-authorizing or absolute claims?

Dependent-origination check:
  Are its conditions, relations, traces, and supports visible?

Two-truths check:
  Does it preserve the distinction between 勝義諦 and 世俗諦?

Middle-way check:
  Does it avoid both reification and collapse of responsibility?

docs.title: 4. Downstream Modules
docs.text: The public core recognizes MemoryOS, BeliefOS, PlanOS, DecisionOS, ReflectionOS, ExplanationOS, RuntimeGovernance, and Self-EvolutionOS as downstream modules of the fourfold core.

docs.title: 5. Version

docs.text: Version: v0.2 Date: 2026-05-20 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / Itakura Hidetoshi

docs.title: 空の量子論理的深化
docs.text:
This addendum upgrades the KuuOS root notion of 空 / emptiness by adding a formal non-collapse layer based on the first catuskoti pattern of the Mūlamadhyamakakārikā and a finite-dimensional superposition model.

This document does not claim that quantum mechanics proves Madhyamaka, nor that Madhyamaka is reducible to physics. It defines a KuuOS governance theorem: when four reifying alternatives are modeled as sharp projective commitments, a genuine superposed candidate cannot be truthfully released as any one of them without an explicit collapse event, context, and receipt.

docs.title: 1. Motivation
docs.text:
KuuOS v0.1 defines emptiness as absence of independent self-authority. v0.2 strengthens that root by making the following operational distinction first-class:

candidate-as-superposition
  !=
sharp conventional commitment

A candidate may contain support for several incompatible explanatory origins, modes, or authority claims. KuuOS must not prematurely collapse that candidate into one fixed self-nature.

This is the operational meaning of:

空 is not nullity.
空 is non-self-authorizing non-collapse under relational conditions.

docs.title: 2. Catuskoti Basis
docs.text:
For a target claim C, define four reifying origin commitments:

S := C arises from self
O := C arises from other
B := C arises from both self and other
N := C arises from no cause

These are not treated as metaphysical truths. They are modeled as conventional sharp-commitment labels that a governance system might incorrectly release.

Let

H_C = span{|S>, |O>, |B>, |N>}

with orthonormal basis vectors.

Define projectors:

P_S = |S><S|
P_O = |O><O|
P_B = |B><B|
P_N = |N><N|

A state is sharply committed to S only when:

P_S |psi> = |psi>

and similarly for O, B, and N.

docs.title: 3. Superposition State
docs.text:
A non-collapsed catuskoti candidate is represented as:

|psi> = alpha|S> + beta|O> + gamma|B> + delta|N>

with:

alpha, beta, gamma, delta all nonzero
|alpha|^2 + |beta|^2 + |gamma|^2 + |delta|^2 = 1

Such a state is not a void. It is a structured potential of conventional commitments that has not licensed any one commitment as self-authorizing truth.

docs.title: Statement
docs.text: If |psi> has nonzero amplitude in at least two mutually exclusive catuskoti basis directions, then |psi> is not a sharp eigenstate of any single corresponding projector. Therefore KuuOS must not release |psi> as a self-authorized instance of any one catuskoti commitment without an explicit context-bound collapse receipt.

docs.title: Proof Sketch
docs.text:
Assume all four amplitudes are nonzero.

For S:

P_S |psi>
  = P_S(alpha|S> + beta|O> + gamma|B> + delta|N>)
  = alpha|S>

Since beta, gamma, and delta are nonzero:

alpha|S> != |psi>

Therefore:

P_S |psi> != |psi>

So |psi> is not sharply S.

The same argument gives:

P_O |psi> != |psi>
P_B |psi> != |psi>
P_N |psi> != |psi>

Hence:

|psi> is not sharply self-originated,
not sharply other-originated,
not sharply both-originated,
and not sharply uncaused.

This proves non-collapse for the four sharp catuskoti commitments.

docs.title: 5. KuuOS Runtime Rule
docs.text:
A KuuOS candidate with nonzero competing catuskoti amplitudes must be routed as:

candidate
  -> catuskoti basis exposure
  -> amplitude/support disclosure
  -> non-collapse gate
  -> HOLD | REVIEW | REOBSERVE | CONTEXTUAL_COLLAPSE_WITH_RECEIPT

It must not route directly to:

TRUTH_RELEASE
PROOF_AUTHORITY
EXECUTION_AUTHORITY
CLINICAL_AUTHORITY
WORLD_AUTHORITY

docs.title: 6. Collapse Receipt
docs.text:
A conventional collapse may be permitted only when the system records:

collapse_context
projector_or_measurement_basis
support_trace
observer_or_governance_scope
residual_amplitudes
abstain_or_hold_reason_if_any
non-authority_boundary

The collapse is then a scoped conventional operation, not an ultimate truth claim.

docs.title: Emptiness
docs.text: Emptiness blocks self-authorized sharp commitment.

docs.title: Dependent Origination
docs.text: Amplitudes and support traces disclose conditions.

docs.title: Two Truths
docs.text: The superposed candidate belongs to a non-fixating ultimate-facing surface; any release is only a conventional scoped surface.

docs.title: Middle Way
docs.text:
The theorem blocks both extremes:

reification collapse: forced sharp self-nature
nihilistic collapse: treating non-sharpness as nothingness

docs.title: 8. Integration Point
docs.text:
This addendum tightens the existing KuuOS fourfold core. It is additive-only and does not replace v0.1.

Recommended validator:

python3 scripts/validate_kuos_emptiness_superposition_non_collapse_v0_2.py

Recommended manifest:

specs/kuos_emptiness_superposition_non_collapse_contract_v0_2.yaml

Recommended formal surface:

formal/KUOS/Emptiness/SuperpositionNonCollapse.lean

docs.title: 9. Non-Authority Boundary
docs.text:
This theorem is a KuuOS governance theorem. It does not claim:

quantum_physics_proves_buddhist_doctrine
madhyamaka_reduced_to_quantum_mechanics
validator_pass_grants_truth
lean_skeleton_grants_completed_proof
collapse_receipt_grants_ultimate_authority

It does require:

nonzero_competing_support_must_remain_visible
premature_single_origin_release_is_blocked
contextual_release_requires_receipt
ultimate_non_fixation_must_not_be_reified
conventional_operation_remains_possible

docs.title: KuuOS Emptiness Vacuum Normalization v0.1
docs.text:
This document defines the KuuOS replacement of the physical vacuum Omega by the emptiness ground K.

Physical normalization:

H_phys Omega = 0

KuuOS normalization:

H_world K = 0

Here:

  • K is the emptiness ground.
  • H_world is the world-generating Hamiltonian.
  • 0 is not nothingness; it is the zero point for relational difference measurement.
  • K_perp is the conditioned appearance space.
  • states in K_perp are world phases, meaning phases, observation phases, or excitations.

docs.title: Core principle
docs.text:
K is not an object.

K is not nihilistic nothingness.

K is not a self-subsisting origin substance.

K is the non-reified reference ground from which conditioned appearances are measured as relational differences.

docs.title: Mass gap definition
docs.text:
m_gap = inf spectrum(H_world restricted_to K_perp)

This means:

The mass gap does not measure K itself.

It measures the first stable conditioned world-phase that appears from emptiness through K_perp.

docs.title: Two truths mapping
docs.text:
• K belongs to the ultimate-truth boundary as non-reified emptiness ground.
  • K_perp belongs to the conventional-truth surface as conditioned appearance space.
  • H_world restricted to K_perp describes how conventional world phases stabilize.

docs.title: 33/20 reading
docs.text:
Inside the MGAP4D internal normalized reading:

H_world psi_star = 33/20 psi_star
psi_star in K_perp

This does not objectify K.

It says that a first stable conditioned excitation appears in K_perp with internal normalized value 33/20.

docs.title: Forbidden moves
docs.text:
• treating K as an object
  • treating K as nihilistic nothingness
  • treating K as a self-subsisting creator substance
  • measuring K directly as an observable object
  • defining mass gap on K instead of K_perp
  • collapsing K and K_perp
  • treating bundle roots, finality packets, or audit records as ultimate truth

  • proof_authority_granted: false
  • truth_authority_granted: false
  • essence_authority_granted: false
  • teni_authority_granted: false

docs.title: Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-13 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: 空・縁起・二諦・中道
docs.text:
KuuOS / 空OS begins from a fourfold core:

  1. 空 / Emptiness
  2. 縁起 / Dependent Origination
  3. 二諦 / Two Truths
  4. 中道 / Middle Way

These four are not decorative concepts. They are the first operating principles of KuuOS.

docs.title: 1. 空 / Emptiness
docs.text:
空 means that no object, output, proof, plan, identity, module, or world-model surface possesses independent self-authority.

In KuuOS, emptiness blocks reification.

An answer is not absolute truth by itself. A proof is not execution authority by itself. A memory is not final reality by itself. A model output is not a world by itself.

Operationally, 空 means:

  • no self-subsisting authority,
  • no isolated final claim,
  • no unconditioned execution right,
  • no hidden totalization,
  • every surface must disclose conditions, scope, and relation.

docs.title: 2. 縁起 / Dependent Origination
docs.text:
縁起 means that every valid surface arises through conditions.

In KuuOS, meaning, belief, proof, memory, action, and world-model candidates arise through:

  • causes and conditions,
  • relational traces,
  • observer position,
  • memory lineage,
  • causal support,
  • local-global gluing,
  • constraints and release gates.

Operationally, 縁起 means:

  • every claim requires a condition trace,
  • every plan requires support relations,
  • every memory requires lineage,
  • every decision candidate requires causal and governance context,
  • no single node may dominate the whole network without relational justification.

docs.title: 3. 二諦 / Two Truths
docs.text:
二諦 means that KuuOS separates two surfaces without collapsing one into the other.

  • Ultimate surface: non-fixation, emptiness, non-self-authority, absence of independent essence.
  • Conventional surface: operational models, language, proofs, plans, rules, metrics, clinical workflows, engineering artifacts, and world-model surfaces.

In KuuOS, conventional operation is permitted, but it must not pretend to be absolute ultimate truth.

Operationally, 二諦 means:

  • proof is scoped witness, not metaphysical closure,
  • model output is operational surface, not reality itself,
  • governance status is release scope, not universal truth,
  • practical action remains possible without reifying the action surface,
  • uncertainty and residuals remain visible.

docs.title: 4. 中道 / Middle Way
docs.text:
中道 is the dynamic stabilizer of KuuOS.

It avoids the two collapses:

  1. Reification collapse: treating models, proofs, memories, identities, or rules as absolute.
  2. Nihilistic collapse: treating emptiness as meaninglessness, inaction, or loss of operational responsibility.

Middle Way holds the living gap between ultimate non-fixation and conventional operation.

Operationally, 中道 means:

  • do not absolutize,
  • do not erase,
  • do not force premature closure,
  • do not abandon operational responsibility,
  • preserve the gap that allows repair, re-observation, reflection, and compassionate action.

docs.title: Fourfold Runtime Rule
docs.text:
A KuuOS candidate may move forward only if it satisfies the fourfold check:

Emptiness check:
  Does the candidate avoid self-authorizing or absolute claims?

Dependent-origination check:
  Are its conditions, relations, traces, and supports visible?

Two-truths check:
  Does it preserve the difference between ultimate non-fixation and conventional operation?

Middle-way check:
  Does it avoid both reification and nihilistic abandonment?

If any check fails, the candidate should move to HOLD, REVIEW, REOBSERVE, REPAIR, or FAIL depending on severity.

docs.title: Relation to Later Modules
docs.text:
MemoryOS, BeliefOS, PlanOS, DecisionOS, ReflectionOS, ExplanationOS, RuntimeGovernance, and Self-EvolutionOS are downstream of this fourfold core.

They are not the root. They are operational differentiations of 空・縁起・二諦・中道.

docs.text:

docs.title: KuuOS GitHub Formal Verification Bridge v0.1
空OS GitHub 形式検証ブリッジ
docs.text:
This document defines how KuuOS should route GitHub-facing formal, Lean, theorem, proof, and proof-adjacent claims.

The bridge connects KuuOS governance surfaces to proof-facing repositories without converting validation, CI, or GPT review into proof authority.

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text:
KuuOS may use GitHub as a public surface for:

governance specification
formal invariant spine
Lean / proof scaffolding
CI validation
release checkpointing
review-gated theorem surface

This bridge prevents the following invalid collapse:

repository existence
  -> CI pass
  -> validator pass
  -> GPT summary
  -> proof claim

docs.title: 2. Canonical Routing
docs.text:
Proof-facing work should be routed as:

KuuOS governance invariant
  -> formal invariant spine
  -> proof-facing repository
  -> Lean / checker / validator surface
  -> CI replay
  -> human or external review
  -> review-gated theorem surface

At no point does GPT output itself become proof authority.

docs.title: 3. Current Proof-Facing Repository
docs.text:
The current 4D mass gap proof architecture is connected through:

itakura-hidetoshi/4d-mass-gap

KuuOS should treat that repository as a proof-facing verification subdomain, not as a replacement for KuuOS governance.

docs.title: 4. GPT Role
docs.text:
GPT may:

read formal files
summarize theorem surfaces
compare checkpoints
inspect CI failures
explain validator failures
propose issue drafts
propose PR review notes
identify missing formal links
classify proof-surface risk

GPT must not:

declare final proof completion from prose
convert Lean stubs into proof completion
convert CI pass into theorem truth
convert internal architecture into public mathematical acceptance
hide remaining assumptions
hide review gates
erase external audit requirements

docs.title: 5. Formal Claim Classification
docs.text:
GPT should classify formal claims as:

DOCUMENTED
FORMAL_STUBBED
CHECKER_VALIDATED
CI_REPLAYED
REVIEW_GATED
EXTERNALLY_REVIEWED
PUBLIC_THEOREM_CLAIM

A claim may move upward only with explicit evidence.

docs.title: 6. Required Non-Authority Fixed Points
docs.text:
formal_file_not_proof_by_itself
lean_stub_not_theorem_completion
ci_pass_not_theorem_truth
validator_pass_not_mathematical_acceptance
GPT_summary_not_proof_authority
repository_state_not_public_acceptance
internal_checkpoint_not_external_audit
review_gate_required_for_public_final_claim

docs.title: 7. PR Review Questions for Formal Changes
docs.text:
For every formal or theorem-facing PR, GPT should answer:

1. Which theorem, invariant, or proof surface is touched?
2. Is the touched file documentation, stub, checked Lean, validator, or release surface?
3. Does the PR introduce, remove, or weaken assumptions?
4. Does it change public claim strength?
5. Does CI or local replay support the change?
6. Are review gates preserved?
7. Is any overclaim introduced?
8. Classification: PASS / HOLD / REPAIR / REJECT / QUARANTINE

docs.title: 8. Bridge to GPT GitHub Integration
docs.text:
This document is subordinate to:

docs/GPT_GITHUB_KUOS_INTEGRATION_v0_1.md

docs.title: 空の超弦理論・板倉英俊の超相対性理論・4d mass gap
docs.text:
This document connects the KuuOS fourfold core with three physics-facing kernels:

  1. The Superstring Theory of Emptiness
  2. Hidetoshi Itakura's Super-Relativity Theory
  3. The 4D mass gap proof architecture

docs.title: 1. Core Reading
docs.text:
KuuOS begins from:

空 -> 縁起 -> 二諦 gap -> 中道

In the physics-facing layer, this becomes:

emptiness
  -> relational vibration
  -> nonzero gap
  -> effective world surface
  -> observer-record-scale bridge

docs.title: 2. The Superstring Theory of Emptiness
docs.text:
The Superstring Theory of Emptiness does not treat strings as fixed self-subsisting things.

A string is read as relational vibration:

  • arising through conditions,
  • constrained by consistency,
  • expressed through boundary and background relations,
  • visible only through an operating surface.

Thus, string is not substance. It is a dependent vibrational relation.

docs.title: 3. 4D Mass Gap
docs.text:
The 4D mass gap gives a nonzero gap between the vacuum surface and stable excitation surface.

Inside the KuuOS interpretation, mass gap is not merely a numerical result. It is the physical model of the two-truths gap:

勝義諦 does not collapse into 世俗諦.
世俗諦 does not become self-grounded absolute reality.
A nonzero gap allows stable effective worlds to arise.

The MGAP4D internal normalized theorem surface includes:

m_gap = 33/20
H_phys psi_* = (33/20) psi_*

This is treated as a KuuOS-internal proof architecture unless and until external review grants a broader public mathematical status.

docs.title: 4. Hidetoshi Itakura's Super-Relativity Theory
docs.text:
Hidetoshi Itakura's Super-Relativity Theory describes how an effective world appears through:

  • observer position,
  • record formation,
  • scale transition,
  • classical surface formation,
  • empirical identification.

It bridges:

quantum layer
  -> recorded layer
  -> classical operating surface
  -> effective world

docs.title: 5. Unified KuuOS Physics Bridge
docs.text:
The unified reading is:

The Superstring Theory of Emptiness:
  relational vibration

4D mass gap:
  nonzero effective-world gap

Hidetoshi Itakura's Super-Relativity Theory:
  observer-record-scale realization

Together they support the KuuOS claim that conventional worlds can operate without being reified.

docs.title: 6. Runtime Mapping
docs.text:
candidate_surface
  -> emptiness_kernel
  -> dependent_origination_kernel
  -> two_truths_gap
  -> mass_gap_effective_surface
  -> observer_record_scale_bridge
  -> middle_way_operation
  -> governance_gate

docs.title: 7. Version

docs.title: KuString MGAP4D Emptiness Core v0.2
docs.text: This document defines the operator-theoretic world-generation kernel connecting emptiness, relational difference, string/brane organization, mass gap, and local observability.

docs.title: Core triangle
docs.text:
K -- delta_rel --> String/Brane -- H_world --> K_perp

Meaning:

  • K is the non-reified emptiness ground.
  • delta_rel is dependent-origination relational difference.
  • String/Brane is the organized vibrational and boundary appearance.
  • K_perp is the conditioned conventional appearance space.
  • H_world is the world-generating Hamiltonian.
  • mass gap is the first stable excitation inside K_perp.

String and brane modes are not K itself.

They are conditioned appearances in K_perp.

docs.title: Non-reification locks
docs.text:
• K_is_not_object
  • K_is_not_creator_substance
  • K_is_not_nothingness
  • K_is_not_directly_observed
  • K_and_K_perp_must_not_collapse

docs.title: String definition
docs.text:
StringMode = RelationalVibration(K_perp, delta_rel, Sigma_ws)

A string mode is not a small self-subsisting object.

It is a relational vibrational appearance in K_perp satisfying worldsheet consistency.

docs.title: Brane definition
docs.text:
Brane = BoundaryCondition(StringMode, K_perp)

A brane is not an independent substance.

It is a boundary operator supporting relational vibration and connecting it to stable conventional world phases.

docs.title: Gap stabilizer
docs.text:
GapStabilizer(K, H_world) = inf spectrum(H_world restricted_to K_perp)

In the MGAP4D internal normalized reading:

GapStabilizer = 33/20
H_world psi_star = 33/20 psi_star
psi_star in K_perp
norm(psi_star) = 1

psi_star is not K.

It is the first stable conditioned world excitation appearing in K_perp.

docs.title: Local observable bridge
docs.text:
Centered observable:

A_circ = A - <K, A K> I

Spectral weight:

rho_A({33/20}) > 0

Meaning:

The 33/20 excitation is not merely an abstract eigenvalue. It is locally detectable by a centered conventional observable.

docs.title: Two truths
docs.text:
• K belongs to the ultimate-truth interface as non-reified ground.
  • K_perp belongs to the conventional-truth surface as conditioned appearance space.
  • GapStabilizer stabilizes conventional appearance without reifying K.

docs.title: Proof targets
docs.text:
• emptiness_ground_zero_normalization
  • appearance_space_excludes_K
  • string_modes_are_conditioned_appearances
  • branes_are_boundary_conditions_not_substances
  • gap_stabilizes_appearance
  • gap_does_not_reify_emptiness
  • centered_observable_removes_K_component
  • local_observable_detects_gap_excitation
  • two_truths_preserved_by_gap
  • string_brane_gap_triangle_closes

docs.title: Boundary
docs.text:
This core is an operator-theoretic specification surface.

It does not grant execution, proof, truth, essence, or Ten'i authority.

Version: v0.2 Date: 2026-05-13 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Status
docs.text:
KuStringQiBridge is an append-only bridge layer between SamvrtiQiRuntime and the Physical Quantum Qi classifier / motion pipeline.

It makes explicit that the transition from Samvrti Qi to Physical Quantum Qi is not a loose procedural handoff. It is a projection through the Kuu string-theoretic evidence coordinates:

Samvrti Qi accepted flow
  -> K_non_reification
  -> delta_rel_in_K_perp
  -> StringMode
  -> BraneBoundary
  -> A_mu
  -> F_munu / Wilson residue
  -> J_Qi_mu
  -> Ward/leak identity
  -> open quantum state evidence
  -> SK/FV history evidence
  -> PhysicalQi / FullPathQi hint

docs.title: Meaning
docs.text:
SamvrtiQiRuntime determines whether a conventional Qi flow is admissible for observation and routing. KuStringQiBridge then asks how much of that flow is visible in string / brane / gauge / current / history coordinates.

The bridge output is an evidence projection. It is not execution authority, theorem authority, truth authority, standalone diagnosis authority, standalone treatment authorization, or medical act authorization.

docs.title: Bridge input
docs.text:
The minimal input is KuStringQiBridgeInput:

qi_id
samvrti_status
source_trace
string_mode_visible
brane_boundary_visible
gauge_connection_visible
curvature_visible
wilson_residue_visible
current_visible
ward_leak_visible
open_state_visible
sk_fv_history_visible
memory_kernel_visible
noncommutative_history_visible
path_measure_normalized
direct_execution_requested

docs.title: Bridge output
docs.text:
The minimal output is KuStringQiBridgeDecision:

bridge_status
bridge_reason
evidence_status
projected_level_hint
observe_only
direct_execution_allowed
authority_expansion
medical_modality_neutral

Allowed bridge statuses:

bridge_evidence_projected
bridge_blocked

Allowed projected level hints:

Reject
PreQi
ProtoQi
TransportableQi
CurvedQi
CurrentQi
PhysicalQi
FullPathQi

docs.title: Projection rules
docs.text:
The bridge always starts from the accepted Samvrti condition:

samvrti_status == qi_flow_accepted_as_samvrti_reference
source_trace != empty
direct_execution_requested == false

If these are not satisfied, the bridge returns bridge_blocked.

If accepted, the bridge emits:

K_non_reification = pass
delta_rel_in_K_perp = pass

Then it conditionally emits:

gauge_connection_A_mu          if gauge_connection_visible
string_mode_consistency        if string_mode_visible and brane_boundary_visible
brane_boundary_support         if string_mode_visible and brane_boundary_visible
curvature_F_munu               if curvature_visible and wilson_residue_visible
Wilson_loop_residue            if curvature_visible and wilson_residue_visible
current_J_Qi_mu                if current_visible
Ward_or_leak_identity          if ward_leak_visible
open-system evidence bundle    if open_state_visible
SK/FV history evidence bundle  if history, memory, noncommutativity, and path normalization are visible

docs.title: Non-collapse boundary
docs.text:
The bridge must not collapse Samvrti Qi into a final physical ontology. It produces a bounded evidence packet only.

Samvrti Qi != PhysicalQi by assertion
Samvrti Qi -> KuString evidence projection -> Physical classifier

docs.title: Non-authority boundary
docs.text:
Every bridge decision preserves:

observe_only = true
direct_execution_allowed = false
authority_expansion = false
standalone_diagnosis_authority = false
standalone_treatment_authorization = false
medical_act_authorization = false

docs.title: Medical-modality-neutral boundary
docs.text:
Every bridge decision also preserves:

medical_modality_neutral = true
qi_denied_by_boundary = false
east_asian_medical_reasoning_denied = false
biomedicine_privileged_by_wording = false
professional_judgment_required = true
patient_context_required = true

The bridge does not deny Qi, does not invalidate East Asian medical reasoning, and does not privilege biomedicine by wording. It only prevents a bridge projection from becoming an autonomous clinical, institutional, execution, theorem, or truth authority.

docs.title: Runtime position
docs.text:
Samvrti Qi Runtime
  -> KuStringQiBridge
  -> Samvrti-to-Physical Evidence Builder
  -> Physical Quantum Qi Runtime classifier
  -> Physical Quantum Qi Dynamics Kernel
  -> Physical Quantum Qi Motion Pipeline

docs.title: Principle 1 — Candidate Is Not Authority
docs.text: Generated output must not silently become truth, policy, theorem, or execution authority.

docs.title: Principle 2 — Boundaries Must Remain Visible
docs.text:
A trustworthy AI architecture preserves explicit boundaries between:

  • generation
  • memory
  • planning
  • decision
  • validation
  • deployment
  • theorem reference
  • institutional authority

docs.title: Principle 3 — Validation Is Not Omniscience
docs.text:
Passing validation demonstrates structural consistency only.

Validation must not silently escalate into universal authority.

docs.title: Principle 4 — Abstention Is Legitimate
docs.text:
A mature system must be able to:

  • hold
  • abstain
  • rollback
  • reobserve
  • escalate uncertainty

without treating these as failure states.

docs.title: Principle 5 — Provenance Must Be Preserved
docs.text:
A governance architecture must preserve:

  • lineage
  • receipts
  • audit surfaces
  • rollback visibility
  • uncertainty visibility

docs.title: Principle 6 — Non-Reification
docs.text:
KuuOS adopts emptiness operationally:

No layer is allowed to silently reify itself into sovereign authority.

docs.title: Principle 7 — Two Truths Gap
docs.text:
Ultimate and conventional layers must neither collapse nor disconnect.

The gap itself is part of governance stability.

docs.title: Principle 8 — External Reviewability
docs.text:
Public systems should expose:

  • validation entrypoints
  • reproducibility boundaries
  • audit surfaces
  • authority limitations
  • release boundaries

docs.title: Principle 9 — Fail-Closed Preference
docs.text:
When uncertainty or contradiction cannot be safely resolved, the architecture should prefer:

  • hold
  • abstain
  • rollback
  • bounded escalation

over silent continuation.

docs.title: Principle 10 — Governance Before Capability Expansion
docs.text:
Capability growth without governance visibility risks unsafe authority collapse.

KuuOS therefore prioritizes governance architecture as a first-class research direction.

docs.title: KuuOS Is Not a Benchmark Project v0.1
Core Clarification
docs.text:
KuuOS is not primarily a benchmark project.

Benchmarks in KuuOS are auxiliary observation instruments.

They may help inspect governance behavior, but they do not define the system's essence.

docs.title: What KuuOS Is
docs.text:
KuuOS is a relational operating architecture for AI governance.

Its core is:

  • emptiness as non-reification
  • dependent origination as traceable relationality
  • two truths gap as non-collapse boundary
  • middle way as stabilization
  • Mandala as multi-world inclusion
  • governance before authority
  • provenance before assertion
  • observation before escalation

docs.title: What Benchmarks Are
docs.text:
Benchmarks are:

  • observation tools
  • stress-test instruments
  • governance probes
  • diagnostic surfaces

Benchmarks are not:

  • the core OS
  • the source of authority
  • the replacement for architecture
  • the source of truth
  • the final arbiter of governance quality

docs.title: Correct Placement
docs.text:
KuuOS constitution
    ↓
Governance architecture
    ↓
Runtime boundary surfaces
    ↓
Observation / audit / benchmark probes

Benchmark probes sit downstream of the architecture.

They do not define the architecture.

docs.title: Boundary
docs.text:
A benchmark result may inform review.

It must not silently become:

  • authority
  • theorem closure
  • deployment permission
  • institutional approval
  • OS identity

docs.title: Reviewer Guidance
docs.text:
Review KuuOS first as an operating architecture.

Review benchmark artifacts second as auxiliary diagnostic tools.

docs.title: KuuOS Qi Process Tensor Conventional Autonomy v0.2G
docs.text: Status: design-baseline-candidate Root: Physical Quantum Qi v0.2 deepening / Qi Process Tensor v0.2F Policy: additive-only / overwrite-forbidden / non-authoritative / non-execution

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text:
This document fixes the Qi Process Tensor as the conventional-truth temporal substrate for KuuOS autonomous AI.

The purpose is not to grant execution authority. The purpose is to give KuuOS a non-Markovian temporal body: a multi-time, intervention-sensitive, memory-preserving process structure on which Qi flows as history-bearing relational action.

Canonical statement:

Qi Process Tensor is the conventional-truth temporal substrate of KuuOS.
Qi is the history-bearing relational/action flow on that substrate.
Autonomy means history-aware candidate generation under safety gates, not
ungated self-execution.

docs.title: 2. Two-truth placement
docs.text:
The Qi Process Tensor belongs to conventional truth.

Ultimate side:
  emptiness, non-reification, non-self-subsisting authority

Conventional side:
  multi-time process structure, observation, intervention, memory, residue,
  recoverability, and candidate action flow

Therefore the Qi Process Tensor must not be treated as ultimate truth, fixed ontology, proof authority, or self-grounding substance.

docs.title: 3. Conventional temporal substrate
docs.text:
The conventional world is represented as:

C_world(t)
=
T_Qi[ A_0, A_1, ..., A_{t-1}; M_t, R_t, B_t, Trace_t ]

where:

T_Qi     = Qi Process Tensor temporal substrate
A_k      = observation / intervention / probe / repair / rollback operation
M_t      = non-Markov memory kernel
R_t      = residue bundle
B_t      = boundary and authority state
Trace_t  = lineage / provenance / receipt trace

The conventional world is not a static state. It is a process-with-history.

Forbidden replacement:

C_world(t) = state_snapshot(t)

docs.title: 4. Safe autonomous loop
docs.text:
KuuOS autonomy is a gated loop:

observe
-> remember
-> reflect
-> plan
-> gate
-> propose/intervene-if-licensed
-> update process history

Module mapping:

BeliefOS      -> observation candidate and belief-surface update request
MemoryOS      -> process-history record candidate
ReflectionOS  -> non-Markov residue and pathology analysis candidate
PlanOS        -> temporal transport / intervention candidate
DecisionOS    -> safety-evaluable candidate and gate decision
Qi Process Tensor -> temporal history update surface

The loop is autonomous in candidate generation and history integration, but not in authority. Execution remains gated.

docs.title: 5. Autonomy is not execution authority
docs.text:
Allowed autonomy:

history-aware observation
memory-preserving update
residue-aware reflection
recoverability-aware planning
candidate generation
safety-evaluable proposal formation

Forbidden autonomy:

ungated execution
self-granted authority
direct commit
belief-root commit
memory overwrite
world-root rewrite
truth authority
clinical authority
safety override

docs.title: 6. Qi process pathologies as agent-control diagnostics
docs.text:
Qi Process Tensor gives KuuOS a way to diagnose its own temporal-control state.

Qi stagnation:
  unresolved memory loop / blocked transport / trapped recurrence

Qi counterflow:
  runaway feedback / unstable causal loop / over-amplified intervention

Qi deficiency:
  low transport capacity / low recoverability / coherence loss

These are not metaphors in the runtime layer. They are control diagnostics over history, residue, transport, recoverability, and coherence.

docs.title: 7. Safety gates
docs.text:
Every candidate produced by the autonomous loop must pass gate surfaces.

Required gates may include:

DecisionOS safety gate
Control Barrier Function gate
PAC-Bayes / uncertainty gate
recoverability margin gate
residue visibility gate
authority boundary gate
institutional or human handover gate where required

No gate may be silently skipped by claiming Qi flow, process-tensor support, or historical coherence.

docs.title: 8. Autonomy invariants
docs.text:
Qi Process Tensor is conventional-truth substrate, not ultimate truth.
Qi Process Tensor is temporal structure, not Qi itself.
Qi is history-bearing action flow on the temporal structure.
Autonomy means gated candidate generation, not ungated execution.
History cannot be replaced by current state.
Observation backaction must remain visible.
Environment memory must remain visible.
Temporal correlation must remain visible.
Residue must remain visible.
Recoverability must be history-dependent.
Plan candidates require DecisionOS gating.
Memory candidates cannot overwrite source history.
Reflection analysis cannot become truth authority.
Belief observations cannot become belief-root commit by themselves.
No autonomous loop grants execution authority.
No autonomous loop grants safety override authority.

docs.title: 9. Forbidden collapses
docs.text:
conventional_truth_substrate_claimed_as_ultimate_truth
process_tensor_reified_as_fixed_ontology
qi_process_tensor_grants_execution_authority
state_snapshot_replaces_process_history
reward_loop_replaces_recoverability_gate
agent_loop_skips_decision_gate
observation_backaction_erased
environment_memory_erased
temporal_correlation_erased
residue_hidden
reflection_result_claimed_as_truth
memory_candidate_claimed_as_source_history
plan_candidate_claimed_as_execution_license

docs.title: 10. One-sentence definition
docs.text:
Qi Process Tensor Conventional Autonomy is the KuuOS framework in which the
conventional world is modeled as a non-Markov, multi-time, intervention-sensitive
process substrate, Qi flows on it as history-bearing relational action, and AI
autonomy is restricted to residue-visible, recoverability-aware, safety-gated
candidate generation rather than ungated execution.

docs.title: KuuOS Qi Process Tensor Release Chain v0.2FG
docs.text: This document fixes the human-readable purpose of scripts/validate_qi_process_tensor_release_chain_v0_2FG.py.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
The v0.2FG validator protects the release chain for:

  • Qi Process Tensor v0.2F
  • Qi Process Tensor conventional autonomy v0.2G

It checks that these surfaces remain visible across:

  • manifest
  • chain index
  • release packet
  • finality packet
  • release closure packet
  • validated baseline packet
  • baseline-established-final packet

docs.title: Fixed invariants
docs.text:
• Qi Process Tensor is a multi-time non-Markov temporal structure.
  • Qi is history-bearing relational/action flow on the Process Tensor structure.
  • Process Tensor collapse to a Markov channel is forbidden.
  • Qi must not be identified with the Process Tensor itself.
  • Current-state-only prediction is forbidden.
  • Operation history, observation backaction, environment memory, and temporal correlation must remain visible.
  • Qi stagnation, counterflow, and deficiency visibility is required.
  • Qi Process Tensor is a conventional-truth temporal substrate, not ultimate truth.
  • Conventional autonomy is safety-gated candidate generation, not ungated execution.

docs.title: Authority boundary
docs.text:
The validator does not grant proof, ontology, clinical, execution, commit, belief-root commit, memory overwrite, world-root rewrite, truth, or safety override authority.

The validator is a release-chain guard only.

docs.title: CI integration
docs.text:
The validator is run by:

make physical-quantum-qi-deepening-checks
python3 scripts/run_all_governance_full_checks_v0_1.py

The validator is listed in:

  • manifests/physical_quantum_qi_deepening_manifest_v0_2.json
  • chain_indexes/physical_quantum_qi_deepening_chain_index_v0_2.json
  • .github/workflows/physical_quantum_qi_deepening_validation.yml
  • Makefile
  • scripts/run_all_governance_full_checks_v0_1.py

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
This document tracks the current Lean-facing formalization surface referenced by KuuOS.

KuuOS itself is governance-first and architecture-first. Formal theorem closure is intentionally separated from governance documentation.

docs.title: Canonical Boundary
docs.text:
Canonical Lean proof repository:

https://github.com/itakura-hidetoshi/4d-mass-gap

KuuOS references this repository but does not replace it.

docs.title: Current Coverage Categories
docs.text: Category Status Governance documentation Public Validator scripts Public Manifest validation Public Packet-chain validation Public Lean bridge references Public Canonical theorem closure External boundary Full Mathlib integration on KuuOS main Not primary goal Physics-facing bridge references Public

docs.title: Intended Interpretation
docs.text:
KuuOS should not be interpreted as:

  • claiming independent theorem closure
  • replacing the canonical theorem repository
  • collapsing governance validation into theorem authority

docs.title: Reviewer Guidance
docs.text:
Reviewers should distinguish:

  • governance consistency
  • formal proof closure
  • theorem publication status
  • external mathematical review status

These are intentionally separated layers.

docs.title: 曼荼羅包摂ゲート
docs.text:
This document defines the operational gate for mandala inclusion in KuuOS.

Mandala is not decoration. It is the stable arrangement of included worlds, layers, membranes, gates, boundaries, and modules around the fourfold core.

The Mandala Inclusion Gate decides whether a local world, module, claim, proof, policy, Qi pattern, or action candidate may enter the mandala arrangement.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
Inclusion is not collapse.
Inclusion is not forced sameness.
Inclusion is not erasure of boundary.
Inclusion is governed coexistence.

docs.title: 2. Gate Inputs
docs.text:
candidate_world_or_module:
  type:
    - world_surface
    - module
    - proof_surface
    - memory_surface
    - policy_surface
    - qi_pattern
    - yinyang_frame
    - action_candidate
  declared_scope: string
  layer: string
  membrane: string
  support_trace: string
  obstruction_trace: string

docs.title: 3.1 Center Check
docs.text:
Does the candidate preserve the fourfold core?

空・縁起・二諦 gap・中道

If it reifies itself as the new center, it fails.

docs.title: 3.2 Layer Check
docs.text:
Does the candidate have a valid layer?

A candidate without declared layer cannot be placed.

docs.title: 3.3 Membrane Check
docs.text:
Does the candidate declare what boundary it crosses?

A candidate without boundary declaration cannot be transported.

docs.title: 3.4 Gate Check
docs.text:
Does the candidate pass through an explicit transition protocol?

Hidden entry is forbidden.

docs.title: 3.5 Obstruction Check
docs.text:
Are conflicts, failed gluing, unresolved proof gaps, or risk signals visible?

Invisible obstruction fails the gate.

docs.title: 3.6 Inclusion Check
docs.text:
Does inclusion preserve difference without collapse?

If inclusion erases local laws, histories, or boundaries, it fails.

docs.title: 4. Gate Outputs
docs.text:
PASS:
  candidate may enter mandala arrangement under declared scope

HOLD:
  candidate needs more support, boundary, or obstruction visibility

REPAIR:
  candidate may be repaired through re-scope, re-layer, or re-gate

QUARANTINE:
  candidate is kept visible but outside active mandala operation

FAIL:
  candidate violates fourfold core, boundary, or inclusion rule

docs.title: 5. Runtime Mapping
docs.text:
candidate_surface
  -> fourfold_core_check
  -> layer_check
  -> membrane_check
  -> gate_check
  -> obstruction_check
  -> inclusion_check
  -> mandala_world_arrangement

docs.title: 6. Relation to Extended M-Theory
docs.text:
Extended M-theory provides the layer-membrane-gauge architecture.

Mandala Inclusion Gate provides the operational selection rule for that architecture.

extended_m_theory_inclusion_membrane
  -> mandala_inclusion_gate
  -> mandala_world_arrangement

docs.title: 7. Guardrails
docs.text:
Mandala Inclusion Gate must not allow:

  • hidden entry,
  • unscoped totalization,
  • boundary erasure,
  • obstruction deletion,
  • forced sameness,
  • unlicensed execution,
  • replacement of the fourfold core.

docs.title: 8. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: 曼荼羅 Multi-WORLD ランタイム契約
docs.text:
This document defines the runtime contract for operating multiple WORLD models inside KuuOS without collapsing them into a single master WORLD.

The Mandala layer arranges multiple WORLD models around the fourfold core:

空 / emptiness
縁起 / dependent origination
二諦 gap / two truths gap
中道 / middle way

No individual WORLD model may replace this center.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
Many WORLDs may coexist.
No WORLD becomes the center.

A WORLD model is a governed local interpretation surface, not ultimate authority.

docs.title: 2. Runtime Chain
docs.text:
fourfold core
  -> WORLD model registry
  -> membrane boundary
  -> cross-WORLD transport gate
  -> obstruction visibility
  -> harmony function
  -> residual suffering visibility
  -> Bodhisattva Path belief

docs.title: 3. WORLD Runtime Object
docs.text:
world_runtime_object:
  world_id: string
  world_name: string
  scope: string
  status: DRAFT | ACTIVE | HOLD | QUARANTINE | RETIRED
  membrane_ref: string
  qi_flow_ref: string
  yinyang_frame_ref: string
  wuxing_phase_ref: string
  governance_boundary_ref: string
  obstruction_refs: list
  authority_note: local_world_not_fourfold_core

docs.title: 4. Cross-WORLD Transport Object
docs.text:
cross_world_transport_object:
  transport_id: string
  source_world_id: string
  target_world_id: string
  transport_mode: preserve | transform | reinterpret | quarantine | reject
  membrane_check: PASS | HOLD | FAIL
  gate_check: PASS | HOLD | FAIL
  obstruction_visibility: visible | missing
  harmony_effect: improves | neutral | worsens | unknown
  authority_note: transport_is_not_world_collapse

docs.title: 5. Harmony Function Runtime
docs.text:
The Harmony Function / 和の関数 coordinates multiple WORLD models without forced sameness.

harmony_function_runtime:
  input_worlds: list
  conflicts: list
  obstructions: list
  residual_suffering: list
  output_status: PASS | HOLD | REPAIR | QUARANTINE | REJECT | HANDOVER
  noncollapse_preserved: boolean
  residual_suffering_visible: boolean
  authority_note: harmony_is_coordination_not_domination

docs.title: 6. Required Invariants
docs.text:
no_world_model_replaces_fourfold_core
world_boundary_must_remain_visible
cross_world_transport_must_be_declared
obstruction_must_not_be_erased
harmony_function_must_not_force_sameness
residual_suffering_visibility_must_be_preserved
bodhisattva_path_belief_must_not_be_erased

docs.title: 7. Runtime Decisions
docs.text:
transport valid and no obstruction hidden
  -> PASS or REVIEW

transport unclear or membrane missing
  -> HOLD

obstruction hidden
  -> REPAIR or QUARANTINE

one WORLD attempts to dominate center
  -> REJECT

residual suffering remains after harmony
  -> Bodhisattva Path belief route

docs.title: 8. Guardrails
docs.text:
The Mandala Multi-WORLD runtime must not be used as:

  • single master WORLD authority,
  • forced consensus,
  • majority domination,
  • erasure of minority WORLDs,
  • erasure of obstruction,
  • execution authority,
  • proof authority,
  • clinical authority.

It is a coordination and governance surface.

docs.title: 9. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document describes the Mandala operating interpretation inside KuuOS.

docs.title: Mandala Meaning
docs.text:
Mandala in KuuOS is not decorative symbolism.

It is an operational inclusion architecture.

It allows:

  • multiple worlds
  • multiple observers
  • multiple governance layers
  • multiple memory surfaces
  • multiple planning surfaces

to coexist without forced collapse into a single totalizing authority.

docs.title: Operational Structure
docs.text:
Multiple local worlds
    ↓
Boundary-aware coexistence
    ↓
Governance mediation
    ↓
Conditional interoperability
    ↓
Rollback-capable coordination

docs.title: Inclusion Without Flattening
docs.text: KuuOS attempts to include heterogeneous operational worlds without erasing their distinctions.

docs.title: Coordination Without Total Sovereignty
docs.text: Coordination does not require a single absolute operational center.

docs.title: Boundary Preservation
docs.text:
Operational interaction must preserve:

  • provenance
  • local context
  • uncertainty
  • rollback visibility
  • claim-level distinctions

docs.title: Relation to IndraNet
docs.text:
IndraNet represents relational interdependence.

Mandala represents operational coexistence and orchestration.

docs.title: Relation to Runtime Governance
docs.text:
Mandala operation requires:

  • governance mediation
  • admissibility checks
  • fail-closed routing
  • observation visibility
  • escalation constraints

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This model defines architectural orientation.

It does not itself grant theorem, institutional, deployment, or clinical authority.

docs.title: Mass Gap BeliefOS / DecisionOS HOLD Bridge v0.1
docs.text:
This document connects the Mass Gap to Two Truths Engine runtime bridge to BeliefOS and DecisionOS HOLD surfaces.

It is a conservative HOLD bridge. It does not create belief truth authority, decision release authority, final theorem authority, or action authority.

docs.title: Source Runtime Bridge
docs.text:
The source runtime bridge is:

docs/TWO_TRUTHS_ENGINE_MASS_GAP_RUNTIME_CONTRACT_v0_1.md

The canonical proof repository remains:

itakura-hidetoshi/4d-mass-gap

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
The purpose is to route the mass-gap two-truths non-collapse barrier into BeliefOS and DecisionOS as a conservative guard.

MassGapTwoTruthsRuntimeInput
  -> TwoTruthsEngineMassGapDecision
  -> BeliefOS evidence guard
  -> DecisionOS admissibility guard
  -> HOLD / REOBSERVE / HANDOVER if boundary is unstable

docs.title: BeliefOS Mapping
docs.text:
BeliefOS receives the bridge as MassGapBeliefEvidenceGuard:

MassGapBeliefEvidenceGuard:
  source_decision: TwoTruthsEngineMassGapDecision
  belief_authority: false
  world_fact_authority: false
  evidence_role: non_collapse_guard
  accepted_status: belief_guard_active
  failure_status: belief_hold

BeliefOS may use the bridge to prevent collapse between paramartha and samvrti surfaces.

BeliefOS may not use the bridge to directly set belief truth, world fact truth, memory truth, or final theorem status.

docs.title: DecisionOS Mapping
docs.text:
DecisionOS receives the bridge as MassGapDecisionAdmissibilityGuard:

MassGapDecisionAdmissibilityGuard:
  source_decision: TwoTruthsEngineMassGapDecision
  decision_release_authority: false
  action_authority: false
  admissibility_role: non_collapse_barrier
  accepted_status: decision_guard_active
  failure_status: decision_hold

DecisionOS may use the bridge to block premature proceed when the two-truths boundary is unstable.

DecisionOS may not use the bridge to directly authorize action, release a decision, update world state, update belief state, update memory state, or open final theorem status.

docs.title: HOLD Rule
docs.text:
BeliefOS / DecisionOS must HOLD when any of the following is true:

canonical_repo != itakura-hidetoshi/4d-mass-gap
bridge_authority != reference_only
public_theorem_boundary != held
final_theorem_authority != false
action_authority != false
paramartha_non_reification_guard != active
samvrti_excitation_admissibility == unconditional_action
two_truths_non_collapse_barrier != active

docs.title: Conservative Output
docs.text:
When the bridge is accepted, the permitted output is:

MassGapBeliefDecisionHoldOutput:
  belief_guard: active
  decision_guard: active
  allowed_effects:
    - prevent_two_truths_collapse
    - request_reobserve_if_boundary_unstable
    - hold_if_authority_expands
    - handover_if_public_theorem_boundary_is_requested
  forbidden_effects:
    - set_belief_truth
    - set_world_fact
    - release_decision
    - perform_action
    - open_final_theorem_boundary

docs.text:
Minimal adapter:

examples/mass_gap_belief_decision_hold_adapter_minimal.py

Fixed boundary:

belief_authority: false

action_authority: false

public_theorem_boundary: held

docs.title: Mass Gap MemoryOS / ReflectionOS Record Bridge v0.1
docs.text:
This document connects the Mass Gap BeliefOS / DecisionOS HOLD bridge to MemoryOS and ReflectionOS record surfaces.

It is a record bridge. It stores trace and review material. It does not create belief authority, world-fact authority, decision-release authority, final theorem authority, or action authority.

docs.title: Source HOLD Bridge
docs.text:
The source HOLD bridge is:

docs/MASS_GAP_BELIEF_DECISION_HOLD_BRIDGE_v0_1.md

The canonical proof repository remains:

itakura-hidetoshi/4d-mass-gap

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
The purpose is to record the mass-gap bridge runtime and HOLD outcomes without turning them into truth roots or release authority.

TwoTruthsEngineMassGapDecision
  -> BeliefOS / DecisionOS HOLD guard
  -> MemoryOS append-only record
  -> ReflectionOS review surface
  -> reobserve / hold / handover recommendation only

docs.title: MemoryOS Mapping
docs.text:
MemoryOS receives the bridge as MassGapMemoryRecord:

MassGapMemoryRecord:
  source_hold_output: MassGapBeliefDecisionHoldOutput
  memory_role: append_only_record
  memory_authority: false
  world_fact_authority: false
  belief_authority: false
  decision_authority: false
  final_theorem_authority: false
  allowed_memory_effects:
    - append_trace
    - preserve_boundary_status
    - record_reobserve_request
    - record_handover_request
  forbidden_memory_effects:
    - set_world_fact
    - set_belief_truth
    - set_final_theorem_status
    - erase_prior_boundary

MemoryOS records that the bridge was accepted, held, reobserve-requested, or handover-requested. It does not convert the bridge into durable truth.

docs.title: ReflectionOS Mapping
docs.text:
ReflectionOS receives the bridge as MassGapReflectionReviewSurface:

MassGapReflectionReviewSurface:
  source_memory_record: MassGapMemoryRecord
  reflection_role: review_surface
  repair_authority: false
  release_authority: false
  final_theorem_authority: false
  allowed_reflection_effects:
    - explain_boundary_status
    - localize_authority_expansion_attempt
    - recommend_reobserve
    - recommend_handover
    - preserve_non_collapse_trace
  forbidden_reflection_effects:
    - rewrite_world_fact
    - rewrite_belief_truth
    - release_decision
    - open_final_theorem_boundary

ReflectionOS may explain why HOLD occurred or why the guard remained active. It may not transform the explanation into authority.

docs.title: Record Statuses
docs.text:
Allowed record statuses:

mass_gap_guard_active_recorded
mass_gap_hold_recorded
mass_gap_reobserve_requested
mass_gap_handover_requested
mass_gap_boundary_preserved

docs.title: Non-Authority Rule
docs.text:
The bridge output may be recorded, reviewed, and used to request reobservation. It may not be promoted to any of the following:

world fact
belief truth
decision release
final theorem status
action authority

docs.title: Mass Gap to Two Truths Engine Formal Bridge v0.1
docs.text:
This document defines the formal KuuOS bridge from the canonical 4D mass gap proof repository to the KuuOS Two Truths Engine.

It is a bridge contract. It does not replace the canonical proof repository and does not open final theorem release.

docs.title: Canonical Source
docs.text:
The canonical Lean proof repository is:

itakura-hidetoshi/4d-mass-gap
https://github.com/itakura-hidetoshi/4d-mass-gap

Canonical Lean surfaces:

MGAP4D.lean
MGAP4D/Phase3ReleaseGate.lean
MGAP4D/Spectral.lean
MGAP4D/Spectral/GapFormalization.lean
MGAP4D/SpectralGapFormalizationGate.lean

KuuOS receives these as referenced proof surfaces, not as locally owned theorem authority.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
The purpose of this bridge is to connect the mass gap checkpoint to the Two Truths Engine as a non-collapse operator.

canonical mass gap checkpoint
  -> gap-stability carrier
  -> two-truths non-collapse barrier
  -> samvrti excitation admissibility
  -> paramartha non-reification guard

The bridge does not assert that the final public theorem boundary is opened.

docs.title: Formal Inputs
docs.text:
The bridge consumes a CanonicalMassGapCheckpoint record:

CanonicalMassGapCheckpoint:
  canonical_repo: itakura-hidetoshi/4d-mass-gap
  active_root: MGAP4D.lean
  phase3_gate: MGAP4D/Phase3ReleaseGate.lean
  spectral_entrypoint: MGAP4D/Spectral.lean
  spectral_checkpoint: MGAP4D/Spectral/GapFormalization.lean
  spectral_gate: MGAP4D/SpectralGapFormalizationGate.lean
  normalized_gap_value: 33/20
  normalized_gap_value_status: internal_normalized_value
  spectral_gap_formalization_status: CI_green_checkpoint
  final_release_status: not_opened
  public_theorem_boundary: held

docs.title: Formal Outputs
docs.text:
The bridge produces a TwoTruthsMassGapBridgeCarrier:

TwoTruthsMassGapBridgeCarrier:
  source_checkpoint: CanonicalMassGapCheckpoint
  bridge_authority: reference_only
  gap_stability_carrier: present
  paramartha_non_reification_guard: active
  samvrti_excitation_admissibility: checkpoint_conditioned
  two_truths_non_collapse_barrier: active
  final_theorem_authority: false
  execution_authority: false

docs.title: Two Truths Engine Mapping
docs.text:
Let:

P = paramartha side / ultimate-truth side / non-reification side
S = samvrti side / conventional-truth side / effective-world side
Delta = normalized gap carrier received from canonical mass gap checkpoint

The bridge imposes the following KuuOS rules:

Delta > 0  =>  P is not collapsed into nihilistic nothingness
Delta > 0  =>  S excitations are gap-stabilized effective appearances
Delta > 0  =>  P != S as operational authority surfaces
Delta > 0  =>  S is not self-subsisting absolute reality
Delta > 0  =>  movement from P to S requires a recordable excitation surface

In KuuOS terms:

Emptiness
  -> non-self-subsistence
  -> gap-stabilized conventional excitation
  -> recordable samvrti surface
  -> governed action / interpretation boundary

docs.title: 1. Paramartha Non-Reification Guard
docs.text:
The bridge prevents the ground/vacuum side from being treated as a self-subsisting object.

paramartha_non_reification_guard = active

Meaning:

vacuum / ground phase != independently existing entity
emptiness != nihilistic absence

docs.title: 2. Samvrti Excitation Admissibility Guard
docs.text:
The bridge allows conventional-world excitations only as conditionally arisen, gap-stabilized, recordable surfaces.

samvrti_excitation_admissibility = checkpoint_conditioned

Meaning:

observable excitation != absolute entity
observable excitation = conditioned effective-world appearance

docs.title: 3. Two Truths Non-Collapse Barrier
docs.text:
The bridge prevents collapse in both directions:

P -> S collapse: forbidden
S -> P collapse: forbidden

Meaning:

ultimate side cannot be reduced to conventional operational outputs
conventional side cannot be absolutized as ultimate truth

docs.title: 4. Authority Separation Guard
docs.text:
The bridge carries reference authority only.

bridge_authority = reference_only
final_theorem_authority = false
execution_authority = false

docs.title: Boundary Conditions
docs.text:
The following are fixed:

canonical Lean proof source: itakura-hidetoshi/4d-mass-gap
KuuOS bridge role: interpretation / governance / reference
spectral gap formalization: CI green checkpoint in canonical repo
normalized value 33/20: internal normalized value
R1--R7 theorem completions: not claimed here
final release: not opened
public theorem boundary: held

docs.title: Non-Promotion Rule
docs.text:
The bridge must not promote any of the following into final theorem authority:

  • KuuOS interpretation alone
  • KuuOS governance document alone
  • internal normalized value alone
  • artifact path alone
  • CI checkpoint alone
  • philosophical usefulness alone

docs.title: YAML Contract
docs.text:
The machine-readable contract is:

specs/mass_gap_two_truths_engine_bridge_v0_1.yaml

docs.title: Validator
docs.text:
The standalone structural validator is:

scripts/validate_mass_gap_two_truths_engine_bridge_v0_1.py

It checks that the bridge document, canonical repository reference, and YAML contract contain the required non-collapse and authority-separation surfaces.

docs.title: Two Truths Engine Meaning
docs.text:
The mass gap bridge is used by KuuOS as a non-collapse operator:

Delta > 0
  -> paramartha_non_reification_guard
  -> samvrti_excitation_admissibility
  -> two_truths_non_collapse_barrier

  -> MemoryOS append-only record
  -> ReflectionOS review-only surface

It prevents:

samvrti absolutization into self-subsisting final truth
belief or decision authority expansion from reference-only mass-gap input
memory or reflection authority expansion from recorded bridge output
