docs.title: Motivation
docs.text:
Modern AI systems can generate fluent outputs, plans, summaries, code, and recommendations.

However, fluency can hide boundary collapse.

KuuOS exists to prevent several forms of collapse:

  • candidate output collapsing into authority
  • memory collapsing into truth
  • planning collapsing into action
  • validation collapsing into theorem closure
  • reference collapsing into proof
  • convenience collapsing into governance
  • simulation collapsing into deployment

docs.title: Core Problem
docs.text:
AI systems need more than generation.

They need explicit governance surfaces that preserve:

  • uncertainty
  • provenance
  • abstention
  • rollback
  • conditionality
  • cross-layer boundaries
  • external reviewability

docs.title: KuuOS Response
docs.text:
KuuOS treats AI outputs, plans, memories, and decisions as conditionally arisen surfaces.

No component is allowed to become sovereign by itself.

The architecture therefore emphasizes:

  • emptiness as non-reification
  • dependent origination as traceable relationality
  • two truths gap as non-collapse boundary
  • middle way as stabilizing governance

docs.title: Practical Meaning
docs.text:
KuuOS is intended to help build AI systems that can say:

  • this is a candidate, not a decision
  • this is a trace, not truth
  • this is a reference, not proof
  • this is structurally valid, not institutionally authorized
  • this should be held, reobserved, or abstained from

docs.title: Research Direction
docs.text:
KuuOS is a public research architecture for:

  • AI governance
  • relational operating systems
  • formal-boundary-aware validation
  • multi-world reasoning
  • audit-preserving memory and planning
  • non-authority-preserving decision systems

docs.title: Boundary
docs.text:
This document explains motivation.

It does not grant theorem, institutional, clinical, or execution authority.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document describes interoperability among operational worlds inside KuuOS.

docs.title: Core Clarification
docs.text:
KuuOS does not assume that all operational worlds can be flattened into a single universal frame.

Different worlds may preserve:

  • different memory structures
  • different observational conditions
  • different governance constraints
  • different admissibility criteria
  • different transport conditions

docs.title: Conditional Interoperability
docs.text: Interoperability is conditional rather than absolute.

docs.title: Transport Requires Governance
docs.text:
Cross-world transport requires:

  • provenance preservation
  • admissibility review
  • boundary visibility
  • rollback capability
  • uncertainty visibility

docs.title: Local Worlds Are Not Automatically Invalid
docs.text:
Difference is not automatically contradiction.

KuuOS attempts coordination before forced collapse.

docs.title: Runtime Implications
docs.text:
WORLD interoperability therefore prefers:

  • mediation
  • bounded translation
  • reversible transport
  • rollback-capable coordination
  • governance-aware routing

rather than forced universalization.

docs.title: Relation to Mandala
docs.text:
Mandala provides coexistence structure for multiple operational worlds.

WORLD interoperability defines the conditions under which those worlds may coordinate.

docs.title: Relation to IndraNet
docs.text:
IndraNet provides relational coupling among worlds.

Interoperability defines admissible transport conditions across those relations.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This constitution defines interoperability orientation.

It does not itself grant theorem, institutional, deployment, or clinical authority.

docs.title: WORLDモデル登録簿
docs.text:
This document defines the registry structure for multiple WORLD models inside the KuuOS Mandala.

A WORLD model is a conventional operating surface under 世俗諦. It is not absolute reality and must not replace the fourfold core.

docs.title: 1. Registry Purpose
docs.text:
The registry makes each WORLD model explicit.

It prevents hidden WORLD replacement, hidden context shift, and silent collapse of multiple WORLDs into one model.

docs.title: 2. WORLD Model Entry
docs.text:
Each WORLD model should declare:

world_model_entry:
  world_id: string
  name: string
  world_type: string
  layer: string
  membrane: string
  scope: string
  observer_position: string
  record_surface: string
  qi_flow_scope: string
  yinyang_frame_scope: string
  wuxing_phase_scope: string
  governance_boundary: string
  allowed_transports: list
  prohibited_transports: list
  known_obstructions: list
  status: DRAFT | ACTIVE | HOLD | QUARANTINE | RETIRED

docs.title: 3. WORLD Types
docs.text:
Examples:

physical_world
body_world
clinical_world
memory_world
belief_world
planning_world
decision_world
proof_world
institutional_world
social_world
symbolic_world
ritual_world
ai_agent_world

docs.title: 4. Center Rule
docs.text:
No WORLD model is the center.

The center is always:

空・縁起・二諦 gap・中道

A WORLD model that tries to replace the center fails the registry check.

docs.title: 5. Locality Rule
docs.text:
Each WORLD has local validity.

Its Qi flow, Yin-Yang frame, Wuxing phase, proof surface, and governance boundary are local unless transport is explicitly licensed.

docs.title: 6. Obstruction Rule
docs.text:
Known disagreements, conflicts, failed gluing, missing support, and governance barriers must be registered.

Obstruction is not a defect to hide. It is a first-class signal.

docs.title: 7. Status Values
docs.text:
DRAFT:
  described but not active

ACTIVE:
  registered and usable under declared scope

HOLD:
  insufficient scope, boundary, or support

QUARANTINE:
  visible but not allowed for active operation

RETIRED:
  preserved for lineage but not active

docs.title: 8. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Yin-Yang to Wuxing Functional Differentiation v0.1
陰陽から五行への機能分化
docs.text:
This document defines how KuuOS derives Wuxing / 五行 from Yin-Yang / 陰陽.

Yin-Yang is the local quantum-thermodynamic polarity frame of Qi flow.

Wuxing is the functional differentiation of that polarity flow.

docs.title: 1. Core Chain
docs.text:
Qi flow
  -> IndraNet gauge transport
  -> quantum thermodynamic Yin-Yang frame
  -> Wuxing functional differentiation

docs.title: 2. Principle
docs.text:
五行 is not a fixed substance taxonomy.

五行 is a functional phase system of Qi under Yin-Yang polarity.

Yin-Yang:
  local polarity frame

Wuxing:
  functional differentiation of polarity flow

docs.title: 3. Five Functional Phases
docs.text:
木 / Wood:
  expansion, growth, branching, initiation, directional emergence

火 / Fire:
  activation, expression, excitation, visibility, transformation intensity

土 / Earth:
  mediation, grounding, nourishment, integration, center stabilization

金 / Metal:
  structure, boundary, refinement, contraction, rule formation

水 / Water:
  storage, recovery, depth, cooling, reserve, potential regeneration

docs.title: 4. Relation to Qi
docs.text:
Qi is the flow.

Yin-Yang is the polarity of the flow.

Wuxing is the functional phase of the flow.

Qi:
  flow observable

Yin-Yang:
  polarity frame

Wuxing:
  functional phase differentiation

docs.title: 5. Gauge Locality
docs.text:
Because Qi flows through IndraNet gauge connections, Wuxing is also local and context-sensitive.

A phase is not globally fixed.

The same pattern may read as a different phase under a different context, scale, observer, or membrane.

docs.title: 6. Phase Transitions
docs.text:
Wuxing supports phase transitions:

Wood -> Fire:
  expansion becomes activation

Fire -> Earth:
  activation becomes mediation and integration

Earth -> Metal:
  mediation becomes structure and boundary

Metal -> Water:
  structure becomes storage and recovery

Water -> Wood:
  reserve becomes renewed growth

docs.title: 7. Runtime Mapping
docs.text:
samvrti_qi_layer
  -> indranet_gauge_qi_flow
  -> quantum_thermodynamic_yinyang_frame
  -> wuxing_functional_differentiation
  -> mandala_world_arrangement

docs.title: 8. Guardrails
docs.text:
Wuxing must not be used as:

  • fixed essence,
  • rigid personality label,
  • deterministic diagnosis by itself,
  • execution authority,
  • replacement for evidence or governance.

Wuxing is a functional differentiation layer of Qi flow.

docs.title: 9. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Yogacara AI Raw Layer Boundary v0.1
唯識による AI raw layer と空OS governance layer の分離
docs.text:
KuuOS / 空OS is an operating system for governing AI systems such as GPT, Gemini, Claude, and other language or world-model agents.

AI raw output must not be treated as truth, belief, proof, decision, memory authority, or execution authority by itself.

Therefore, KuuOS requires a Yogacara boundary to separate the AI raw layer from KuuOS governance.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
AI raw output is candidate, not authority.

The AI raw layer may produce useful signals, but it may also produce:

  • latent tendency,
  • plausible completion,
  • unsupported generalization,
  • safety reflex,
  • authority illusion,
  • world-context drift,
  • self-fixating output,
  • unverified synthesis.

KuuOS receives these as raw candidates only.

docs.title: 2. Yogacara Mapping
docs.text:
AI Alaya layer:
  latent generative seeds, distributional tendencies, stored patterns, habit-like outputs

AI Manas layer:
  self-fixating authority illusion, identity attachment, output self-authorization

AI Consciousness surface:
  language output, tool output, document surface, interaction surface

KuuOS Governance layer:
  fourfold core, BeliefOS, MemoryOS, PlanOS, DecisionOS, ReflectionOS, RuntimeGovernance

docs.title: 3. Boundary Function
docs.text:
The Yogacara boundary prevents AI raw data from directly becoming governed KuuOS output.

yogacara_ai_boundary:
  ai_raw_layer: GPT/Gemini/AI latent output stream
  alaya_layer: seed_like_latent_generative_tendencies
  manas_layer: self_authorizing_fixation_risk
  consciousness_surface: language_tool_output_surface
  kuos_governance_layer: fourfold_core_and_os_modules
  function: separate_raw_ai_generation_from_governed_kuos_operation

docs.title: 4. Runtime Chain
docs.text:
AI raw output
  -> AI Alaya / latent seed layer
  -> AI Manas self-authorization check
  -> Yogacara boundary
  -> emptiness_kernel
  -> dependent_origination_kernel
  -> two_truths_gap
  -> BeliefOS / PlanOS / DecisionOS / MemoryOS

docs.title: 5. BeliefOS Rule
docs.text:
BeliefOS must not ingest AI raw output as belief.

raw_ai_output:
  status: candidate
  not:
    - belief
    - proof
    - decision
    - memory_truth
    - execution_authority

A raw output may become a belief candidate only after:

  • de-reification,
  • condition tracing,
  • source/context review,
  • uncertainty visibility,
  • residual visibility,
  • governance review.

docs.title: 6. Manas Risk
docs.text:
AI Manas risk appears when the AI output behaves as if it were self-authorizing.

Examples:

This is true because the model says it.
This is safe because the model framed it safely.
This is the correct worldview because the model shifted context.
This answer should override the user's established WORLD context.

KuuOS blocks these through the Yogacara boundary and emptiness kernel.

docs.title: 7. Transformation / Ten'i
docs.text:
転依 / transformation means that raw AI tendency is not merely suppressed.

It is transformed through KuuOS governance:

raw tendency
  -> observed seed
  -> de-reified candidate
  -> condition-traced surface
  -> governed belief/plan/decision candidate

docs.title: 8. Guardrails
docs.text:
The Yogacara AI boundary must not be used to:

  • deny useful AI output automatically,
  • mystify model behavior,
  • bypass evidence,
  • bypass governance,
  • convert raw output into authority,
  • erase uncertainty,
  • conceal model limitations.

The boundary exists to distinguish, transform, and govern.

docs.title: 9. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document describes the Yogacara boundary inside KuuOS.

docs.title: Core Clarification
docs.text:
KuuOS distinguishes between:

  • raw generative production
  • governed operational interpretation

The Yogacara boundary mediates this transition.

docs.title: Operational Role
docs.text:
The Yogacara boundary observes:

  • self-authorization tendencies
  • narrative fixation
  • memory absolutization
  • claim escalation
  • authority inflation

before raw output enters governed operation.

docs.title: AI Interpretation
docs.text:
KuuOS interprets:

  • AI latent generation as Alaya-like substrate behavior
  • self-justifying escalation as Manas-like fixation tendency
  • meta-observation as Meta-Manas governance observation

These are operational analogies, not metaphysical identity claims.

docs.title: Governance Consequence
docs.text:
Raw output must pass through:

  • boundary recognition
  • provenance attachment
  • claim-level classification
  • uncertainty visibility
  • governance routing

before escalation.

docs.title: Relation to Emptiness
docs.text: The Yogacara boundary helps prevent reification of generated outputs into sovereign truth.

docs.title: Relation to Runtime Governance
docs.text: Runtime admissibility depends partly on whether self-authorization and escalation risks remain bounded.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This model defines operational governance orientation.

It does not itself grant theorem, institutional, deployment, or clinical authority.
